title: Stomp
summary_theology: Exuberant gospel celebration of freedom in worship, declaring that joy in the Lord is the believer's strength.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy celebration; great for youth events and joyful gatherings.
arrangement_tips: Full gospel band with brass, choir; funk groove. Let the congregation move.

title: Revolution
summary_theology: Calls the church to be agents of transformation in the world, living the revolutionary love of Christ.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for justice-themed or revival services. Choir-driven.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary gospel production; choir and full band. Build to massive climax.

title: Melodies from Heaven
summary_theology: Declares praise as a melody sent from heaven, uniting earthly and celestial worship.
practical_worship_leadership: Beautiful opening anthem; congregation engages with the choir call-and-response.
arrangement_tips: Lush gospel orchestration; choir-led. Piano and strings build beautifully.

title: Hosanna
summary_theology: Celebratory gospel proclamation of Hosanna, rooting the church's praise in the Palm Sunday acclamation.
practical_worship_leadership: Perfect for Palm Sunday or any celebration of Christ's kingship.
arrangement_tips: Gospel choir and full band; march feel with gospel syncopation.

title: Shackles (Praise You)
summary_theology: Testimony of liberation from every chain, responding with praise to the God who sets free.
practical_worship_leadership: Very high energy; cross-cultural appeal. Works in contemporary and gospel settings.
arrangement_tips: R&B gospel feel; driving rhythm, layered vocals. Great for freedom or breakthrough services.

title: Go Get It
summary_theology: Encourages believers to step out in faith and claim the promises God has already given them.
practical_worship_leadership: Motivational and high-energy; great for faith series or youth events.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary gospel R&B; strong rhythm section and harmonies.

title: Yesterday
summary_theology: Declares the forgiveness and newness of life in Christ, releasing the past and embracing God's fresh start.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for New Year, baptism, or new beginnings series.
arrangement_tips: Soulful gospel ballad; piano and smooth rhythm section.

title: We Fall Down
summary_theology: Draws from the heavenly worship of Revelation, leading the church to prostrate before God's holiness.
practical_worship_leadership: Deeply reverent; effective at quieting a congregation into worship.
arrangement_tips: Sparse piano intro; choir enters softly. Build into full proclamation at the bridge.

title: Stand
summary_theology: Gospel anthem of perseverance, declaring that after all has been done, the believer stands by God's grace.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for services on perseverance, grief, or coming through trials.
arrangement_tips: Gospel piano; strong choir presence. The slow build makes the chorus a release.

title: Never Would Have Made It
summary_theology: Deeply personal testimony that without God's faithfulness through trials, survival would have been impossible.
practical_worship_leadership: Very powerful after a time of congregational testimony sharing. Deeply moving.
arrangement_tips: Slow gospel ballad; piano-led with choir swell. Allow the congregation to reflect.

title: Praise Him in Advance
summary_theology: Calls believers to offer praise before seeing the answer, trusting God's faithfulness to fulfill his word.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for faith-building services or when the congregation is facing difficulty.
arrangement_tips: Gospel piano and choir; builds from declaration to full celebration.

title: In the Sanctuary
summary_theology: Celebrates the blessing of gathering in the sanctuary, entering God's presence with praise.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic gospel church opener; congregation knows it well.
arrangement_tips: Traditional gospel piano and choir; joyful and celebratory.

title: For Every Mountain
summary_theology: Testimonial gratitude for every mountain crossed and valley passed through by God's faithful hand.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for testimonial services or Thanksgiving. Very moving.
arrangement_tips: Build from soft piano to full choir. The congregation joins naturally.

title: This Joy
summary_theology: Declares the joy of the Lord as a present reality that the world cannot take away.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic gospel celebration song; works for all ages in gospel contexts.
arrangement_tips: Lively gospel piano; full choir and congregation. March-like feel.

title: Open My Heart
summary_theology: Prayer for God to open and prepare the heart for his transforming presence and truth.
practical_worship_leadership: Soulful and intimate; great at the start of a service to prepare hearts.
arrangement_tips: R&B gospel ballad; piano-led with smooth rhythm section.

title: Still I Rise
summary_theology: Declaration of resurrection resilience, rising through trials by the power of God's sustaining grace.
practical_worship_leadership: Empowering for services on perseverance or healing. Congregation is uplifted.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary gospel; full production with choir. Building from verse to anthem chorus.

title: This Is the Day
summary_theology: Jubilant declaration that this is the day the Lord has made, calling for rejoicing in the present moment.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic opener; every congregation can sing it. Very accessible.
arrangement_tips: Bright gospel piano; add claps and full band. March-like energy.

title: Lord Help Me to Hold Out
summary_theology: Earnest prayer for divine strength to endure to the end, rooted in gospel tradition.
practical_worship_leadership: Traditional gospel; resonates deeply with older congregations.
arrangement_tips: Traditional gospel piano; choir-led. Allow call-and-response.

title: While I'm Waiting
summary_theology: Declares active, worshipful waiting on God even when answers are delayed, trusting his perfect timing.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for seasons of waiting or unanswered prayer. Very honest and accessible.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic and gentle; piano and acoustic guitar. Allow the words to speak.

title: Holy Is the Lord
summary_theology: Declaration drawn from the seraphic vision that the whole earth is filled with God's glory.
practical_worship_leadership: Very popular worship song; congregation engages easily. Great mid-set.
arrangement_tips: Full band; the we stand and lift up our hands call is effective. Build each chorus.

title: Glory to God Forever
summary_theology: Joyful ascription of eternal glory to God from all creation, joining the worship of heaven now.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy praise song; works as an opener or celebratory moment.
arrangement_tips: Full electric production; anthemic chorus. Very singable.

title: Made to Worship
summary_theology: Declares that humanity was made and redeemed specifically to worship God, making praise our highest calling.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for a teaching series on worship itself. Accessible and sing-able.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary rock; full band. Clean and simple arrangement works best.

title: How Great Is Our God
summary_theology: One of the most sung worship songs globally, declaring the incomparable greatness of God in nature and redemption.
practical_worship_leadership: Works in virtually any context worldwide. Can sustain extended worship.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic to full production; the bridge brings in many languages in global settings.

title: Your Grace Is Enough
summary_theology: Celebration of God's all-sufficient grace that is greater than every need and failure.
practical_worship_leadership: Very accessible and loved widely. Good in any part of the service.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary acoustic-electric blend. The congregation picks up the chorus immediately.

title: Mighty to Save
summary_theology: Declares the mighty saving power of God and the light of Christ overcoming darkness.
practical_worship_leadership: One of Hillsong's most globally known songs. Works as an opener or mid-set.
arrangement_tips: Full band; anthemic chorus that congregation projects into. Strong final key change.

title: With Everything
summary_theology: Commitment to worship God with every part of life — heart, soul, mind, and strength.
practical_worship_leadership: Works as a dedication or commissioning song. Congregation connects with the totality language.
arrangement_tips: Builds from sparse acoustic to full production. Pre-chorus can be spoken or sung softly.

title: Touch the Sky
summary_theology: Celebrates the paradox that losing everything for God is gaining everything; surrender becomes soaring.
practical_worship_leadership: Builds to an exhilarating climax. Great for services on total surrender.
arrangement_tips: Full production; the final chorus should feel victorious and free.

title: Captain
summary_theology: Declares Jesus as the captain of salvation, anchoring trust entirely in his leadership.
practical_worship_leadership: Meditative and powerful; works in extended worship or as a closing declaration.
arrangement_tips: Lush production; electric guitar swells. Build from intimate to massive.

title: Miracles
summary_theology: Declares bold faith for miracles, rooted in the nature of a God for whom nothing is impossible.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for healing services or any service on faith and the supernatural.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary rock production; anthemic. Build to the climactic final chorus.

title: Whole Heart (Hold Me Now)
summary_theology: Offers the whole heart to God and rests in the assurance of being held securely by his faithfulness.
practical_worship_leadership: Beautiful for prayer or surrender moments. Works very well with youth.
arrangement_tips: Intimate and lush; starts sparse then builds. Allow the congregation to linger.

title: Prince of Peace
summary_theology: Worships Jesus as the Prince of Peace, declaring his name as the source of true rest in chaos.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for anxious congregations or services on mental health and peace.
arrangement_tips: Atmospheric production; starts very gentle, builds to powerful declaration.

title: So Will I (100 Billion X)
summary_theology: Creation testifies to God's nature and the cross demonstrates his love; the believer joins all creation in response.
practical_worship_leadership: Profound lyrics reward repeated singing. Works in any service on creation or the gospel.
arrangement_tips: Piano-driven with orchestral layers; do not rush. Each verse is a theological journey.

title: Not Today
summary_theology: Defiant declaration of resistance against the enemy, grounded in the victory of Christ.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy declaration song; effective for spiritual warfare themed services.
arrangement_tips: Full rock production; driving rhythm. Congregation can shout the declaration.

title: Sinking Deep
summary_theology: Sinking into the depths of God's love and losing oneself entirely in his presence.
practical_worship_leadership: Works well for young adults or youth worship. Very atmospheric.
arrangement_tips: Electronic elements combined with organic instruments. Builds beautifully.

title: Alive
summary_theology: Declares the resurrection life of Christ now alive in the believer, transforming everything.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy; great for youth and young adult contexts. Easter works perfectly.
arrangement_tips: Electronic-pop production; full energy from the start. Bright and celebratory.

title: Wonder
summary_theology: Stands in wonder at the majesty of creation and the even greater wonder of God's love for humanity.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for outdoor services or services on creation and God's majesty.
arrangement_tips: Sweeping production; lots of space and atmosphere. Congregation reflects as it sings.

title: Let There Be Light
summary_theology: Prays for the light of Christ to break through darkness in lives, cities, and nations.
practical_worship_leadership: Good for missions focus or any service on Christ as the light of the world.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary rock; building arrangement. Works as an opener or sendout.

title: Beneath the Waters (I Will Rise)
summary_theology: Baptism song celebrating the believer's death to self and rising to new life in Christ.
practical_worship_leadership: Perfect for baptism services. Very moving when sung during immersion.
arrangement_tips: Soft and reverent; build from quiet piano to full band at the chorus.

title: This I Believe (The Creed)
summary_theology: Contemporary musical setting of Apostolic faith, giving the congregation words to declare core beliefs.
practical_worship_leadership: Excellent for congregations learning theology or for a series on the creeds.
arrangement_tips: Builds in energy as the creed is declared. Full band; congregation stands and declares.

title: From the Inside Out
summary_theology: Prayer for God's glory to be displayed from the inside out, transforming the inner life first.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for services on inner renewal or sanctification. Congregation loves the bridge.
arrangement_tips: Piano-driven; builds to full band. The outro my soul my life can be extended.

title: Aftermath
summary_theology: Testimony of God's faithfulness after walking through the storm, declaring him worthy of trust.
practical_worship_leadership: Post-trial testimony song. Works for services following difficult seasons.
arrangement_tips: Builds from sparse to full production. Very dynamic.

title: Zion
summary_theology: Celebrates the church as Zion, the mountain of God where his presence dwells and his people gather.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for church anniversary or services on the nature of the church.
arrangement_tips: Full electric production; anthemic. Build to the final declaration.

title: Blessed Be Your Name
summary_theology: Commitment to bless God whether on the road of abundance or through the wilderness of pain.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the great modern worship anthems on trials. Works year-round.
arrangement_tips: Rock feel; acoustic intro, electric build. The though you take away section is very moving.

title: Cry Out to Jesus
summary_theology: Speaks directly to those in pain and loss, pointing them to cry out to Jesus as the only answer.
practical_worship_leadership: Perfect for grief, addiction recovery, or healing-focused services.
arrangement_tips: Soft acoustic ballad; emotional and vulnerable. Let the lyrics lead.

title: Show Me Your Glory
summary_theology: Moses-like prayer for a direct encounter with the glory of God, rooted in desperate longing.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for prayer services or services calling for renewed encounter with God.
arrangement_tips: Southern rock feel; guitar-driven. Build from verse to anthemic chorus.

title: Come Together
summary_theology: Call for the body of Christ to unite in worship and mission, laying down division.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for ecumenical services or church unity themes.
arrangement_tips: Rock anthem; full band. March-like chorus.

title: Your Love Oh Lord
summary_theology: Paraphrase of Psalm 36 declaring the vast, unfailing love of God that reaches beyond the heavens.
practical_worship_leadership: Beautiful and accessible; congregation loves the Psalm text set to music.
arrangement_tips: Electric and acoustic blend; the dynamics shift naturally with the text.

title: God's Not Dead
summary_theology: Defiant proclamation of the living God against secular denial, rooted in the resurrection.
practical_worship_leadership: Very popular with youth and young adults. High energy declaration.
arrangement_tips: Rock anthem; full production with driving drums. Congregation shouts the chorus.

title: We Believe
summary_theology: Contemporary creedal statement of core Christian beliefs, sung as a corporate declaration.
practical_worship_leadership: Very effective for affirmation of faith moments in the service. Congregation loves declaring together.
arrangement_tips: Rock production; builds to full anthem. Congregation stands for the declaration.

title: Shine
summary_theology: Call to let the light of Christ shine through the believer's life into the surrounding darkness.
practical_worship_leadership: Energetic and fun; works for youth and outreach events.
arrangement_tips: Upbeat pop-rock; danceable groove. Very catchy chorus.

title: He Reigns
summary_theology: Envisions all nations joining the worship of heaven, declaring that God reigns over all.
practical_worship_leadership: Perfect for World Missions Sunday or a service on global Christianity.
arrangement_tips: Big anthem feel; full production. Can layer different languages in the bridge.

title: Big House
summary_theology: Joyful picture of heaven as God's big house with rooms for everyone, emphasizing welcome and community.
practical_worship_leadership: Beloved by anyone who grew up in the 90s CCM era; great for youth or nostalgia services.
arrangement_tips: Rock with folk-punk energy; full band, contagious groove.

title: Hands and Feet
summary_theology: Prayer to be made the hands and feet of Christ in the world, serving the marginalized.
practical_worship_leadership: Perfect for missions, justice, or service Sundays. Very challenging and moving.
arrangement_tips: Rock feel; emotional build. The lyrics carry the weight — do not over-produce.

title: I Can Only Imagine
summary_theology: Contemplates what it will be like to stand before God in eternity, overwhelmed with wonder.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the best-selling Christian songs ever. Works for funerals, memorials, and Easter.
arrangement_tips: Piano ballad; do not over-produce. The simplicity is the power.

title: Even If
summary_theology: Honest wrestling with unanswered prayer, arriving at faith in God's goodness even if the miracle does not come.
practical_worship_leadership: Deeply powerful for grief, illness, or chronic struggle. Very honest.
arrangement_tips: Piano and acoustic; emotional. Allow silence before the final chorus.

title: I Am
summary_theology: Declares the true identity of the believer in Christ — not defined by past failures but by who God says they are.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for identity-focused services or any series on grace.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary rock; anthemic chorus. Very accessible.

title: Finally Home
summary_theology: Anticipates arrival in heaven as finally coming home, ending the journey of faith in the presence of God.
practical_worship_leadership: Beautiful for funeral or memorial services. Also works for services on eternal life.
arrangement_tips: Piano ballad with gentle production. The imagery is very accessible.

title: Flood
summary_theology: Desperate prayer from the midst of overwhelming circumstances, clinging to God as the only rescue.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for honesty moments in worship. Resonates deeply in times of crisis.
arrangement_tips: Alternative rock feel; sparse verses, dynamic chorus. Let the tension build.

title: Worlds Apart
summary_theology: Honest confession of the distance between self and Christ, and the willingness to die to self.
practical_worship_leadership: Deeply personal and challenging. Works for services on the cost of discipleship.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic folk-rock; sparse and vulnerable. Not a high-energy song.

title: Dare You to Move
summary_theology: Challenges the listener to take the step of faith and move toward the redemption that awaits.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for evangelistic services or any message on new beginnings.
arrangement_tips: Rock anthem; builds from quiet to massive. Very culturally accessible.

title: This Is Your Life
summary_theology: Challenges the listener to examine whether their life is aligned with its true purpose in God.
practical_worship_leadership: Good for New Year services or any message on purpose and calling.
arrangement_tips: Rock production; energetic. Works for younger audiences especially.

title: How He Loves
summary_theology: John Mark McMillan's meditation on the reckless, overwhelming love of God poured out at the cross.
practical_worship_leadership: Extremely powerful; one of the great contemporary worship songs. Use for soaking or extended worship.
arrangement_tips: Can go acoustic or full electric. The congregational response after the first chorus is organic.

title: All I Need Is You
summary_theology: Declares that God alone is the ultimate need and desire of the soul, above every earthly substitute.
practical_worship_leadership: Intimate and meditative; good for quiet worship segments.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic feel; piano or guitar-driven. Keep it simple.

title: Everything Glorious
summary_theology: Declares God's ability to make everything glorious, transforming the broken into the beautiful.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for any series on redemption or transformation.
arrangement_tips: Electronic elements mixed with acoustic; atmospheric feel. Builds gradually.

title: Never Lost
summary_theology: Declares that God has never lost a battle, never failed his people, and never will.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy anthem for services on God's faithfulness or spiritual victory.
arrangement_tips: Country-gospel fusion; banjo, acoustic guitar, full band. Very singable.

title: There Will Be a Day
summary_theology: Promises that suffering is temporary and a glorious day of resurrection and healing is coming.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for grief, illness, or memorial services. Very comforting.
arrangement_tips: Ballad; build carefully from verse to climactic final chorus.

title: Walk by Faith
summary_theology: Commitment to walk by faith in God's word even when circumstances cannot be seen or understood.
practical_worship_leadership: Good for a series on faith or hearing from God. Very accessible.
arrangement_tips: Rock production; driving acoustic guitar. Congregation engages easily.

title: Do Everything
summary_theology: Challenges believers to glorify God in the mundane tasks of daily life, not just in spiritual activities.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for stewardship or vocational ministry series.
arrangement_tips: Upbeat pop; very singable. Works for any age group.

title: Heaven in the Real World
summary_theology: Calls the church to bring the hope of heaven into the brokenness of the real world through love and service.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for outreach or social justice themed services.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary rock; anthemic. Build to a strong congregational chorus.

title: The Great Divide
summary_theology: Declares that Christ bridged the great divide between sinful humanity and holy God through his sacrifice.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic CCM; works for communion or a sermon on reconciliation.
arrangement_tips: Piano ballad or contemporary arrangement; strong harmony vocals.

title: More Than Anything
summary_theology: Prayer to desire God above every circumstance and blessing, placing relationship with him as the greatest need.
practical_worship_leadership: Works well as a personal surrender moment or before a sermon on priorities.
arrangement_tips: Power ballad; builds emotionally. Strong solo vocalist needed.

title: You Are More
summary_theology: Counters shame and self-condemnation with the truth that identity is more than past mistakes.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for recovery ministries, youth, or any service on identity and grace.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic and emotional; do not over-produce. The vulnerability is the power.

title: Worn
summary_theology: Honest prayer of the exhausted soul, crying out to God for rest and renewed strength.
practical_worship_leadership: Very moving for congregations in difficult seasons. Honesty is refreshing.
arrangement_tips: Spare acoustic; keep it understated. The bridge is powerful.

title: Forgiven
summary_theology: Joyful declaration of forgiveness received, countering the enemy's accusations with truth.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for services on grace or after a confession moment.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary rock; positive and uplifting. Congregation responds well.

title: Everything Comes Alive
summary_theology: Celebrates the life-giving power of Christ's Spirit that brings dead things to life.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy; good for services on the Holy Spirit or new life.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary rock with Irish folk undertones; full band.

title: Magnify
summary_theology: Takes Mary's Magnificat as the model for magnifying God above all circumstances.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for Advent especially, or any service on magnifying God.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary acoustic feel; build to full band. Congregational melody is strong.

title: Fires
summary_theology: Declares trust in God even when walking through fire, believing he is present in the refining process.
practical_worship_leadership: Moving for services on perseverance or walking through hard seasons.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary acoustic-electric; emotional. Build from intimate to anthem.

title: Different
summary_theology: Prayer to be genuinely different from the world, not conformed but transformed by Christ.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for services on holiness or discipleship. Very honest.
arrangement_tips: Country-influenced contemporary; acoustic and warm.

title: You Love Me Anyway
summary_theology: Marvels at the unconditional love of God that pursues and accepts the sinner without condition.
practical_worship_leadership: Very accessible; works for all ages. Great for Easter or grace-focused services.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary acoustic; warm and inviting feel. Congregation joins naturally.

title: joy.
summary_theology: Declares joy as an active choice and weapon against despair, grounded in Christ's victory.
practical_worship_leadership: Very high energy; great for youth and young adults.
arrangement_tips: Anthemic pop; driving percussion. Congregation physically responds.

title: Amen
summary_theology: Declares amen as the total agreement of the believer with everything God is and says.
practical_worship_leadership: Works as a closing dedication or response song.
arrangement_tips: Cinematic pop-rock production; builds to full anthem.

title: He Is
summary_theology: Meditates on the many names and roles of Jesus, building to a declaration of his all-sufficient identity.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for a series on the names of Jesus. Very worshipful.
arrangement_tips: Piano ballad; choir can join at the chorus. Emotional and reflective.

title: Get Down
summary_theology: Calls the congregation to get down on their knees in humble adoration before the Lord.
practical_worship_leadership: High energy and fun; great for youth worship or lively congregations.
arrangement_tips: Rock feel; guitar-driven. Very participatory.

title: Speak Life
themes: life and death in the tongue
summary_theology: Calls believers to use words that build up and give life rather than destroy.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for series on communication, community, or encouragement.
arrangement_tips: Hip-hop influenced pop; accessible for youth. Very upbeat.

title: Move (Keep Walkin)
summary_theology: Encourages believers to keep moving and pressing forward in faith regardless of obstacles.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy motivational song; works for youth events or outreach.
arrangement_tips: Hip-hop pop; energetic production. Great for active worship contexts.

title: You Are
summary_theology: Declaration of the many ways God reveals himself — refuge, life, anchor, strength.
practical_worship_leadership: Contemporary pop-worship; accessible for youth and young adults.
arrangement_tips: Pop production; layered vocals. Bridge is very strong.

title: Brother
summary_theology: Celebrates the depth of brotherhood and Christian community as a reflection of Christ's self-giving love.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for church unity or brotherhood-themed services. Very emotional.
arrangement_tips: Americana rock; acoustic and electric blend. Builds beautifully.

title: Believer
summary_theology: Confident declaration of faith as the defining identity of the believer, not swayed by circumstances.
practical_worship_leadership: Contemporary and accessible; good for confirmation or faith series.
arrangement_tips: Pop-rock feel; singable chorus. Positive and uplifting.

title: Fear Is Not My Future
summary_theology: Bold declaration that fear has no authority over the believer whose identity is secured in Christ.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy declaration; great for services on anxiety, fear, or faith.
arrangement_tips: Full gospel-influenced production; anthemic. Congregation shouts the declaration.

title: Praise (All Day)
summary_theology: Exuberant commitment to praise God all day, every day, as the natural response to his grace.
practical_worship_leadership: Great opener; very high energy and celebratory.
arrangement_tips: Full production; gospel-influenced rhythm section. Congregation participates from the start.

title: Lion
summary_theology: Worships Jesus as the Lion of Judah who has conquered death and reigns victorious.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for spiritual warfare or Easter services.
arrangement_tips: Builds from sparse to full electric; dramatic. Big final declaration.

title: Welcome (Holy Spirit)
summary_theology: Simple invitation for the Holy Spirit to be welcome and move freely in the gathering.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for prayer services or services focused on the Holy Spirit.
arrangement_tips: Sparse and atmospheric; allow room for the Spirit to move. Do not over-produce.

title: Jireh
summary_theology: Declares contentment in God as Jireh the provider, finding sufficiency in him in every circumstance.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the most powerful recent worship songs. Works in extended worship.
arrangement_tips: Can start very sparse; builds beautifully. Allow the bridge to develop organically.

title: All Sufficient Merit
summary_theology: Declares resting in Christ's all-sufficient merit alone for righteousness before God.
practical_worship_leadership: Theologically rich; works well paired with a gospel-centered sermon.
arrangement_tips: Piano-led; restrained production. New hymn feel.

title: Praise the Lord (feat. Brandon Lake)
summary_theology: Corporate invitation to all creation to praise the Lord in unrestrained, joyful worship.
practical_worship_leadership: Works as a celebration opener. Full-energy gospel feel.
arrangement_tips: Gospel-soul production; full choir and band. Let the congregation move freely.

title: Lean Back
summary_theology: Invitation to stop striving and lean back into the trustworthy arms of God.
practical_worship_leadership: Meditative and accessible; great for services on anxiety or trust.
arrangement_tips: Soul-gospel feel; piano and keys. Very groove-oriented. Do not rush.

title: Wait on You
summary_theology: Expresses the posture of waiting on God's timing and trusting his wisdom in seasons of delay.
practical_worship_leadership: Intimate worship; good for extended waiting on God or prayer services.
arrangement_tips: Sparse and atmospheric; contemporary soul feel. Allow extended time.

title: Jubilee (Overflow)
summary_theology: Declares the year of jubilee fulfilled in Christ, bringing freedom and overflow to the captive.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for services on freedom, debt forgiveness, or the year of jubilee.
arrangement_tips: Gospel-soul groove; builds to joyful celebration. Full band and choir.

title: Doesn't Matter
summary_theology: Declares that nothing else matters when one has God — his presence is the ultimate treasure.
practical_worship_leadership: Soul-gospel feel; intimate and powerful.
arrangement_tips: Sparse keys and rhythm; build into the chorus. Allow spontaneous expression.

title: Gratitude
summary_theology: Offers simple, sincere gratitude to God as the purest form of worship in response to his grace.
practical_worship_leadership: Extremely popular; easy to lead. Works in any context.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic-led; straightforward production. Do not over-arrange.

title: Bright as Gold
summary_theology: Declares trust in the refining process of trials, believing God is producing something bright as gold.
practical_worship_leadership: Good for services on perseverance or walking through fire.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary acoustic; builds emotionally. Very honest and moving.

title: The Blessing
summary_theology: Sets the Aaronic blessing from Numbers 6 to music, declaring God's favor and peace over his people.
practical_worship_leadership: Extraordinary for services of blessing, ordination, or sending. Congregation weeps.
arrangement_tips: Starts sparse; layers voices gradually. Allow extended time for the blessing to land.

title: Forever Faithful
summary_theology: Rests in the covenant faithfulness of God that endures even when human faith wavers.
practical_worship_leadership: Accessible and singable; good for general worship or services on trust.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary acoustic-electric; clean arrangement.

title: White Flag
summary_theology: Offers total surrender to God, raising the white flag of yielded will before the King of kings.
practical_worship_leadership: Very powerful for surrender moments. Works as an altar response.
arrangement_tips: Build from sparse acoustic to full anthem. The surrender imagery is very visual.

title: King of the World
summary_theology: Acknowledges the folly of trying to control outcomes, surrendering to the King who holds all things.
practical_worship_leadership: Very relatable; great for services on control, anxiety, or trust.
arrangement_tips: Pop ballad; piano-led with gentle production.

title: Holy Forever
summary_theology: Contemporary setting of the trisagion, joining the eternal declaration of God's holiness.
practical_worship_leadership: Builds beautifully into extended worship. Congregation naturally lingers.
arrangement_tips: Piano-led; allow organic build. The holy holy holy refrain is very powerful.

title: Manifest
summary_theology: Prayer for God to manifest his presence tangibly in the gathering of his people.
practical_worship_leadership: Works well in prayer services or extended worship sets.
arrangement_tips: Atmospheric production; allow space. Build from intimate to full.

title: Another in the Fire
summary_theology: Draws from the story of the three in the furnace, declaring God is present in the fire with his people.
practical_worship_leadership: Very powerful for services on suffering or trial. Congregation often weeps.
arrangement_tips: Build from sparse piano to full production. The story arc of the song needs space.

title: Reckless Love
summary_theology: Meditates on the relentless, chasing love of God who pursues every lost sheep with reckless abandon.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the most sung modern worship songs globally. Allows deep soaking.
arrangement_tips: Can start a cappella or sparse piano; builds into full production. Bridge is powerful.

title: Surrounded (Fight My Battles)
summary_theology: Declares worship as the weapon that fights battles, trusting God fights on behalf of his worshipers.
practical_worship_leadership: Extremely popular; works for spiritual warfare or general worship. Bridge sustains extended worship.
arrangement_tips: Atmospheric and building; the this is how I fight my battles section is very declarative.

title: Forever Reign
summary_theology: Declaration of Christ's eternal reign and the believer's desire to surrender to that reign completely.
practical_worship_leadership: Very popular and congregationally strong. Works as a mid-set surrender song.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary acoustic to full production. The chorus is very singable.

title: Open Arms
summary_theology: Celebrates the open arms of the Father who runs to meet the returning prodigal with grace.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for evangelistic services, baptism, or prodigal-themed series.
arrangement_tips: Warm and inviting production; piano-led. The imagery is very powerful.

title: King (of My Heart)
summary_theology: Meditates on Christ as the supreme king of the heart, sufficient for every need and desire.
practical_worship_leadership: Very popular modern worship song; sustains extended worship well.
arrangement_tips: Can be done very simply or with full production. The lyrics reward slow, reflective singing.

title: Worthy of Your Name
summary_theology: Ascribes all praise and worth to God, declaring him alone worthy of the church's highest praise.
practical_worship_leadership: Very popular Passion anthem; congregation engages deeply.
arrangement_tips: Full production; anthemic. Build to a massive final chorus.

title: Death Was Arrested
summary_theology: Declares the arrest of death and the triumph of the resurrection, rooting the believer's hope in the gospel.
practical_worship_leadership: Perfect for Easter; also works year-round for gospel-centered worship.
arrangement_tips: Full contemporary production; driving rhythm. Build to a climactic ending.

title: All the Poor and Powerless
summary_theology: Calls every broken, marginalized, and powerless person to raise their voice in praise to Jesus.
practical_worship_leadership: Deeply moving; works for justice-themed services or any call to include the marginalized.
arrangement_tips: Build from acoustic quiet to full band. Very powerful dynamics.

title: Spirit of the Living God
summary_theology: Prayer for the Spirit of the living God to fall fresh and transform every area of life.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic that gets updated; very accessible for all generations.
arrangement_tips: Can be sung simply with piano or with full production. Allow repetition.

title: Forever (We Sing Hallelujah)
summary_theology: Weaves the Easter story of creation, fall, and redemption into a rising hallelujah of eternal praise.
practical_worship_leadership: Incredible for Easter; also strong year-round for gospel-centered worship.
arrangement_tips: Builds steadily over 5+ minutes to a massive finish. Very dynamic.

title: More Than Conquerors
summary_theology: Proclaims the believer's identity as more than a conqueror through the overwhelming love of Christ.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for services on identity or spiritual victory.
arrangement_tips: Anthemic rock production; very singable chorus.

title: Way Maker
summary_theology: Live worship version of the beloved Sinach song, declaring God as way-maker and promise-keeper.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the most sung songs globally in recent years. The bridge sustains extended worship.
arrangement_tips: Start sparse; build throughout. The even when I dont see it bridge is very powerful.

title: Well Done
summary_theology: Expresses longing to hear the Father's well done at the end of a life of faithful service.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for ordination, dedication, or end-of-year services. Very moving.
arrangement_tips: Piano-led ballad; emotional. Allow it to breathe.

title: To Worship You I Live
summary_theology: Declares that the entire life is oriented toward worshiping God — worship is not a song but a life.
practical_worship_leadership: Works as a commissioning or dedication song. Very congregational.
arrangement_tips: Gospel-rock feel; full band and choir. Very accessible melody.

title: Good God Almighty
summary_theology: Jubilant declaration of God's goodness woven through all of life and creation.
practical_worship_leadership: Very upbeat and joyful; great opener for a celebration service.
arrangement_tips: Country-gospel fusion; acoustic guitar, keys, full band. Congregation participates enthusiastically.

title: Speak to Me
summary_theology: Prayer for God to speak through his word and Spirit, attentively waiting for his voice.
practical_worship_leadership: Works before a sermon or in a prayer-focused service.
arrangement_tips: Intimate acoustic production; spare and reflective.

title: Hold On to Me
summary_theology: Prayer for God to hold on through the darkest moments, trusting his sustaining grip.
practical_worship_leadership: Very effective for crisis contexts, grief, or anxiety. Congregation receives it deeply.
arrangement_tips: R&B gospel-influenced; piano and soft rhythm. Very emotional.

title: Fill Me Up
summary_theology: Prayer to be filled to overflowing with the Holy Spirit so that the excess blesses others.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for Holy Spirit-focused services or prayer meetings.
arrangement_tips: Gospel R&B; piano and choir. Allows extended soaking.

title: Wide As the Sky
summary_theology: Meditates on the infinite dimensions of God's grace that exceed all measurement.
practical_worship_leadership: Beautiful mid-set worship song; congregation reflects on the immensity of grace.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary acoustic-electric; lush sound. Build across the song.

title: Haneul Wi Joo-nim Kkeseo (God in Heaven)
summary_theology: Korean praise song exalting the God who dwells in heaven yet stoops to dwell with his people.
practical_worship_leadership: Use in Korean-language services or multicultural gatherings.
arrangement_tips: Piano and choir; can be sung in Korean and English together.

title: I Love You Lord (Korean)
summary_theology: Simple Korean declaration of love to God, expressing the heart of devotion in the believer.
practical_worship_leadership: Beautiful for multicultural services; the melody is simple and universal.
arrangement_tips: Piano alone or light guitar. Teach phonetically for non-Korean speakers.

title: Jesus We Need You
summary_theology: Honest prayer of dependence on Jesus as the one in whom all life, breath, and meaning are found.
practical_worship_leadership: Works in Korean or English; very accessible internationally.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary production; piano and electric. Very singable melody.

title: Amazing Grace (Mandarin)
summary_theology: The beloved hymn of grace rendered in Mandarin Chinese for the global Chinese church.
practical_worship_leadership: For Chinese-language services or multilingual settings.
arrangement_tips: Traditional hymn style; or contemporary arrangement. Both work.

title: Zhu Zai Wo Zuo Pang (God Is by My Side)
summary_theology: Mandarin worship song declaring God's constant nearness and comfort to the believer.
practical_worship_leadership: Popular in Chinese-language churches; bridges to English speakers.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary piano-led; light and warm feel.

title: Ku Naikkan Syukur (I Lift My Praise)
summary_theology: Indonesian praise song lifting thanksgiving to God for his mercy and goodness.
practical_worship_leadership: Use in Indonesian-language services or Asian church gatherings.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary pop-worship feel; piano and guitar. Bilingual singing works well.

title: Kay Tagumpay Ka (You Are Victorious)
summary_theology: Tagalog worship anthem declaring the victory of Christ over sin and death.
practical_worship_leadership: For Filipino congregations or multicultural Asian services.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary production; full band. Can be sung bilingually.

title: Tukuinuke Mungu (We Praise You God)
summary_theology: Swahili worship song of praise to God, drawing from East African Christian musical tradition.
practical_worship_leadership: Beautiful for multicultural worship; teach the Swahili phonetically.
arrangement_tips: African percussion, guitar, vocal harmonies. Joyful and participatory.

title: Baba Mungu (Father God)
summary_theology: East African prayer song addressing God as Father, reflecting the intimacy and trust of the Lord's Prayer.
practical_worship_leadership: Use for prayer-focused services; teach congregation the Swahili refrain.
arrangement_tips: Guitar and African percussion; call-and-response works well.

title: Yesu Karibu (Welcome Jesus)
summary_theology: Swahili welcome song inviting the presence of Jesus into the gathering of his people.
practical_worship_leadership: Opening song for Swahili or multicultural services.
arrangement_tips: African rhythm instruments; joyful. Teach melody and simple Swahili refrain.

title: Masithokoze (Let Us Praise)
summary_theology: Zulu praise song calling all people to celebrate and glorify God together.
practical_worship_leadership: Excellent for multicultural or missions services. The rhythm is infectious.
arrangement_tips: Zulu vocals, percussion, antiphonal structure. Teach by ear.

title: Hallelujah (Ngiyabonga)
summary_theology: South African blending of hallelujah with Zulu thanksgiving, celebrating God's goodness.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for multicultural celebrations. Very participatory.
arrangement_tips: African percussion and choir; call-and-response. Can layer with English.

title: Siyahamba (We Are Marching in the Light of God)
summary_theology: Zulu Christian song of joyful marching in the light of God, combining Zulu tradition with gospel faith.
practical_worship_leadership: Beloved globally; great for processionals or children's participation.
arrangement_tips: Percussion-driven; many verses in different languages. Congregation naturally marches.

title: Cristo Te Ama (Christ Loves You)
summary_theology: Simple Spanish declaration of Christ's love, accessible for any Spanish-language context.
practical_worship_leadership: For Spanish-language services or bilingual congregations.
arrangement_tips: Guitar-driven Latin rhythm; warm and inviting. Can be sung in both languages.

title: Dios Incomparable
summary_theology: Spanish worship declaring there is no one like God, incomparable in glory, power, and love.
practical_worship_leadership: Very popular in Latin American churches; also sung internationally.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary Latin worship; piano and guitars. Anthemic chorus.

title: Quiero Conocerte (I Want to Know You)
summary_theology: Intimate Spanish worship song expressing deep longing to know God more fully.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for quiet worship or prayer services in Spanish-language contexts.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic guitar and piano; intimate feel.

title: Santo (Holy)
summary_theology: Spanish worship song drawing from the trisagion vision of Isaiah 6, declaring God's holiness.
practical_worship_leadership: Works in Spanish-language or bilingual services.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary Latin worship; builds from spare to full band.

title: Hosanna (Caribbean Worship)
summary_theology: Caribbean reggae-influenced hosanna celebration declaring Christ as king with infectious joy.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for Palm Sunday or a celebration service with Caribbean influence.
arrangement_tips: Reggae feel; acoustic guitar, percussion. Congregation sways naturally.

title: Abide with Me
summary_theology: Evening hymn of dependence on God's abiding presence through the darkness of death itself.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic for funerals, evensong, or nighttime services. Very moving.
arrangement_tips: Organ or piano; slow and reflective. Four-part choir is beautiful.

title: Day by Day
summary_theology: Medieval prayer for three daily gifts: to see Christ more clearly, love him more dearly, and follow him more nearly.
practical_worship_leadership: Beautiful for daily prayer liturgy; also well-known from the musical Godspell.
arrangement_tips: Simple waltz feel; acoustic guitar or piano. Very gentle.

title: Eternal Father Strong to Save
summary_theology: Navy hymn asking God to protect those in peril on the sea, acknowledging his sovereign power over creation.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic for military or naval services; also used for funerals of veterans.
arrangement_tips: Organ and brass; stately and dignified. Very familiar to military communities.

title: Let All Mortal Flesh Keep Silence
summary_theology: Ancient communion hymn calling for awed silence before the incarnate Lord who comes to give himself.
practical_worship_leadership: Profound for Advent, Communion, or Epiphany. Very ancient and reverent.
arrangement_tips: Unison voices with organ; or a cappella. The mystery should be felt in the arrangement.

title: What Child Is This
summary_theology: Asks and answers the great Advent question: this child in the manger is the King of kings.
practical_worship_leadership: Beautiful for Christmas services; well-known melody (Greensleeves) aids participation.
arrangement_tips: Minor feel on verse; can use acoustic guitar for intimate feel. Choir on chorus.

title: O Come All Ye Faithful
summary_theology: Great Christmas processional calling all the faithful to come and adore the incarnate Lord.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic Christmas Eve opener or processional. Congregation knows every word.
arrangement_tips: Full organ; congregation stands. Latin title Adeste Fideles can be used as verse.

title: Good Christian Men Rejoice
summary_theology: Medieval carol calling all Christians to rejoice with full heart, soul, and voice at Christ's birth.
practical_worship_leadership: Joyful and energetic; great for a lively Christmas service.
arrangement_tips: Brass and organ; full congregation. Very accessible melody.

title: In the Bleak Midwinter
summary_theology: Rossetti's Christmas poem pondering the humble setting of the Incarnation and the appropriate response of offering.
practical_worship_leadership: Beautiful and meditative; works as an intimate Christmas carol or Christmas Eve reading.
arrangement_tips: Simple piano or a cappella; do not rush. Tune Cranham or Gustav Holst setting.

title: Once in Royal David's City
summary_theology: Traces the life of Jesus from manger to heaven, presenting him as the model for human life and holiness.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic Nine Lessons and Carols opener. Beautiful procession as a soloist begins.
arrangement_tips: Unison verse by solo child voice; congregation joins chorus. Organ with gradual build.

title: O Little Town of Bethlehem
summary_theology: Contemplates the quiet miracle of Bethlehem where the great hopes and fears of all the years met in Christ.
practical_worship_leadership: Beloved Christmas carol; works for any Christmas service.
arrangement_tips: Tune Forest Green or St. Louis; soft and intimate. Piano or organ.

title: Lo How a Rose Eer Blooming
summary_theology: Medieval German Christmas carol tracing Christ as the Rose of Sharon prophesied in Isaiah.
practical_worship_leadership: Beautiful for Advent or Christmas Eve. Often sung a cappella or with solo.
arrangement_tips: Four-part a cappella is exquisite; or simple piano. Very slow and tender.

title: Now the Day Is Over
summary_theology: Evening prayer hymn asking for God's blessing through the night, suited for children and evening services.
practical_worship_leadership: Good for children's ministry or evensong worship services.
arrangement_tips: Simple waltz; piano only. Quiet and peaceful.

title: Jesus Shall Reign (Doxology Version)
summary_theology: The classic doxology pairing praise to Father, Son, and Spirit with Watts's missionary vision.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic closing benediction; very familiar to most congregations.
arrangement_tips: Organ; congregation stands for the doxology. Very brief — powerful as a benediction.

title: Stay with Us (Emmaus)
summary_theology: Taizé chant drawing from the Emmaus road prayer, asking the risen Christ to remain with his people.
practical_worship_leadership: Beautiful for Easter evening or any service on the presence of the risen Christ.
arrangement_tips: Repeated ostinato; simple and meditative. Allow 5-10 minutes of repetition.

title: In Manus Tuas (Into Your Hands)
summary_theology: Latin chant of surrender, echoing Christ's words from the cross: into your hands I commend my spirit.
practical_worship_leadership: Profound for Good Friday or any moment of total surrender.
arrangement_tips: A cappella or simple keyboard; very slow. Allow silence after each repetition.

title: Jesus Remember Me
summary_theology: Simple chant echoing the prayer of the penitent thief, expressing humble dependence on Christ's mercy.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for any service on repentance, grace, or Good Friday.
arrangement_tips: Very simple canon; teach to any congregation in 30 seconds. Allow extended repetition.

title: Veni Lumen Cordium (Come Light of Hearts)
summary_theology: Latin Pentecost chant calling for the Holy Spirit to come as light into every heart.
practical_worship_leadership: Use for Pentecost services or any prayer for the Spirit.
arrangement_tips: Flowing chant; repeat slowly. Instruments enter gradually — strings, then full ensemble.

title: Enemy of Apathy
primary_artist: John Bell and the Iona Community
summary_theology: Describes the Holy Spirit as the disturbing, disruptive breath of God who upsets comfortable apathy.
practical_worship_leadership: Prophetically challenging; great for Pentecost or revival services.
arrangement_tips: Celtic folk feel; can be sung a cappella or with simple accompaniment.

title: Take This Moment
summary_theology: Offers each present moment as a gift to God, finding the sacred in the ordinary.
practical_worship_leadership: Good for daily prayer liturgies or stewardship-themed services.
arrangement_tips: Celtic acoustic feel; piano or guitar. Simple and meditative.

title: Lord of All Hopefulness
summary_theology: Follows God's presence through the rhythms of the day from morning to night, asking for joy and strength.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for morning worship or a service on daily Christian life.
arrangement_tips: Tune Slane (same as Be Thou My Vision); flowing and accessible.

title: Thine Is the Glory
summary_theology: Easter victory hymn using the triumphal theme from Handel's Messiah to declare Christ's risen glory.
practical_worship_leadership: Perfect Easter anthem; congregation responds to the familiar Handel melody.
arrangement_tips: Organ and brass; march-like. Full congregation standing.

title: Ye Servants of God
summary_theology: Calls all servants of God to join the heavenly multitude in ceaseless praise to the Lamb.
practical_worship_leadership: Very strong closing hymn or processional. Full of Revelation imagery.
arrangement_tips: Tune Hanover; organ and full congregation. Can add brass.

title: This Is the Feast
summary_theology: The feast of victory for our God; worthy is the Lamb who was slain, drawing on Revelation for communion liturgy.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic Lutheran liturgical piece; beautiful in communion services.
arrangement_tips: Choral with organ; or contemporary arrangement with acoustic instruments.

title: Kyrie Eleison
summary_theology: The ancient three-fold prayer for mercy, used across Orthodox, Catholic, and Protestant traditions.
practical_worship_leadership: Works in any liturgical context; also used in contemporary worship.
arrangement_tips: Can be chanted, harmonized, or done in contemporary style. Very flexible.

title: Gloria in Excelsis Deo
summary_theology: Angels' song at the Nativity, used in liturgy as the great hymn of praise to God.
practical_worship_leadership: Used in multiple contexts — Christmas season or as a liturgical canticle.
arrangement_tips: Traditional Latin setting or contemporary; the Christmas version is very accessible.

title: O Come O Come Emmanuel
summary_theology: Ancient antiphons longing for the Messiah, perfectly capturing the Advent posture of expectant waiting.
practical_worship_leadership: Essential Advent hymn; the minor key captures the longing beautifully.
arrangement_tips: Organ and unison congregation; or contemporary folk arrangement. Allow the minor feel.

title: Holy God We Praise Thy Name
summary_theology: Paraphrase of the ancient Te Deum, joining the cosmic choir of angels in unceasing praise to the Triune God.
practical_worship_leadership: Majestic and traditional; great for special services or Trinity Sunday.
arrangement_tips: Full organ; strong congregational unison. Can add brass.

title: Grace Greater Than Our Sin
summary_theology: Classic hymn declaring that God's grace is always greater than and overcoming of human sin.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for communion or a grace-centered service. The chorus is very strong.
arrangement_tips: Piano or organ; can be done traditionally or with contemporary touch.

title: Not in Me
summary_theology: Declares there is no righteousness in the self; all hope rests on Christ alone.
practical_worship_leadership: Theologically precise; works for Reformed or grace-focused congregations.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary piano and acoustic; clear and focused. Congregation can easily learn it.

title: O Great God
summary_theology: Prayer for God to form Christlikeness in the believer, beginning with a vision of God's holiness.
practical_worship_leadership: Strong for a holiness or sanctification-themed service.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic and restrained; allow the prayer to breathe.

title: I Will Glory in My Redeemer
summary_theology: Rich theological declaration of the believer glorying in Christ alone — his death, resurrection, and reign.
practical_worship_leadership: Theologically rich modern hymn; excellent for a cross-centered service.
arrangement_tips: Piano and choir; builds across three verses. Congregation learns it quickly.

title: Before the Throne of God Above
summary_theology: Draws from Hebrews and Romans to declare the believer's security in Christ's heavenly intercession.
practical_worship_leadership: Very powerful; one of the great theological hymns of the 19th century.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary version with acoustic guitar and piano is very popular. Strong congregational melody.

title: Healer
summary_theology: Declaration of trust in God as healer, clinging to faith even when physical healing has not yet come.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for healing services; also powerful for any honest prayer for healing.
arrangement_tips: Sparse piano; build gently. Allow the congregation to bring their needs.

title: Come to the Well
summary_theology: Invitation to bring every soul-thirst to the living water that only Jesus provides.
practical_worship_leadership: Good for evangelistic services or any service on spiritual hunger and satisfaction.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary rock; warm and inviting. Congregation responds to the invitation.

title: My Lighthouse
summary_theology: God as lighthouse guiding the believer through dark and stormy waters to safe harbor.
practical_worship_leadership: Very joyful and accessible; great for youth or family contexts.
arrangement_tips: Celtic folk feel; acoustic guitar, fiddle, full band. Very fun to lead.

title: Build Your Kingdom Here
summary_theology: Prayer for the kingdom of God to come and transform the streets, cities, and structures of this world.
practical_worship_leadership: Excellent for justice or mission Sundays. Very anthemic.
arrangement_tips: Folk-rock anthem; driving rhythm. Congregation physically responds.

title: Come to the Table
summary_theology: Invitation to all the broken and weary to come to Christ's table where grace is lavishly given.
practical_worship_leadership: Perfect for communion services. The invitation imagery is very powerful.
arrangement_tips: Warm and welcoming production; piano-led. Allow the congregation to move slowly.

title: See What a Morning
summary_theology: Narrates the resurrection morning with theological depth, tracing its cosmic significance for all creation.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the finest Easter hymns of the modern era. Very singable.
arrangement_tips: Three strong verses; build from piano to full band. Use Easter morning for maximum impact.

title: Take Me to the River (Wade in the Water)
summary_theology: African-American spiritual using water imagery to express the liberation and new life found in Christ.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for baptism services or any service on new life in Christ.
arrangement_tips: Spiritual feel; piano, bass, choir. Allow swinging rhythm. Very participatory.

title: God Is So Good
summary_theology: Simple children's praise declaring the goodness of God; pure and accessible for all ages.
practical_worship_leadership: Intergenerational; brings down adult defenses. Works in family services.
arrangement_tips: Very simple; acoustic guitar or piano. Children can lead adults.

title: Father Abraham
summary_theology: Action song celebrating believers as children of Abraham through faith in Christ.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic VBS or Sunday school song; gets everyone moving.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic guitar; all-action participation. No amplification needed.

title: My God Is So Big
summary_theology: Simple declaration of God's unlimited greatness, power, and ability to do all things.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic children's worship; motions make it very participatory.
arrangement_tips: Simple piano or guitar; action motions. Children lead the adults.

title: Jesus Loves Me
summary_theology: The most sung Christian song globally; simple declaration of Christ's love for children and all people.
practical_worship_leadership: Universally known; works in every culture. Karl Barth called it the answer to theology.
arrangement_tips: Simple piano; can do contemporary version. Children lead joyfully.

title: Lord Prepare Me to Be a Sanctuary
summary_theology: Prayer to be made a holy sanctuary for God's presence, purified and set apart for his use.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic camp and small-group worship song. Very accessible.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic guitar or piano; simple. Works beautifully a cappella.

title: We Fall Down
summary_theology: Draws from Revelation 4 and the elders casting their crowns before the Lamb in worshipful submission.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for moments of corporate surrender or Communion. Classic camp song.
arrangement_tips: Simple acoustic; the holy is the Lamb refrain can be sung repeatedly.

title: You Are Good (Spontaneous)
summary_theology: Simple, repeated declaration of God's goodness that allows extended, spontaneous congregational worship.
practical_worship_leadership: Can be sung spontaneously or structured. Very flexible for any worship context.
arrangement_tips: Start with piano; repeat and layer as the worship deepens.

title: Build a Boat
summary_theology: Noah-inspired call to obey God in faith even when the command makes no sense.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for a faith or obedience series.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary pop-rock; upbeat and accessible.

title: You Say
summary_theology: Grounds identity entirely in what God says rather than what the world, circumstances, or self-perception say.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the most popular Christian songs of recent years. Works in virtually any context.
arrangement_tips: Piano-ballad; build carefully. The bridge is very powerful.

title: Beautiful Things
summary_theology: Declares God's ability to make beautiful things out of dust and brokenness.
practical_worship_leadership: Very moving; works for services on redemption, grief recovery, or new beginnings.
arrangement_tips: Indie-folk feel; build from sparse to full. Very honest and vulnerable.

title: All My Tears (Be Washed Away)
summary_theology: Joyful anticipation of heaven where all tears are washed away, embracing death as entry into glory.
practical_worship_leadership: Beautiful for funerals or memorial services. Also works for general Easter hope.
arrangement_tips: Folk simplicity; guitar and soft vocals. Allow the congregation to reflect.

title: Gracefully Broken
summary_theology: Finds the paradox that being gracefully broken before God leads to being lifted and used by his grace.
practical_worship_leadership: Deeply personal; works for moments of corporate surrender or personal ministry.
arrangement_tips: Sparse and intimate; allow the brokenness of the lyrics to be felt.

title: See a Victory
summary_theology: Declares expectation of seeing God's victory in every situation, trusting his faithfulness.
practical_worship_leadership: Very popular recent song; congregation knows it. Works for any service.
arrangement_tips: Builds from sparse to full electric. Very anthemic final chorus.

title: Cornerstone
summary_theology: Uses the Cornerstone imagery to declare Christ as the only secure foundation in life and death.
practical_worship_leadership: Extremely popular globally. Works for any service — communion, funerals, general worship.
arrangement_tips: Start sparse; build to full production. The Edward Mote verse is very powerful.

title: In the River
summary_theology: Uses Ezekiel's river vision to invite total immersion in the Holy Spirit's life-giving flow.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for Holy Spirit-focused services or prayer meetings.
arrangement_tips: Atmospheric production; allow the music to flow like water. Extended instrumental sections.

title: I Speak Jesus
summary_theology: Declares the power inherent in speaking the name of Jesus over every situation and need.
practical_worship_leadership: Very popular recent song; congregation declares the name with power.
arrangement_tips: Start simple; build to full production. The repetition is intentional and effective.

title: Death Came Knocking
summary_theology: Poetic meditation on death coming to the door and finding it already defeated by the resurrection.
practical_worship_leadership: Profound; works for Easter or any service on resurrection hope.
arrangement_tips: Atmospheric and sparse; allow the poetry to breathe.

title: Grateful
summary_theology: Heartfelt gospel gratitude for God's faithfulness through every season of life.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for Thanksgiving or any testimonial service.
arrangement_tips: Gospel piano and choir; warm and celebratory.

title: Something Has to Break
summary_theology: Prayer of faith declaring that something must break through — chains, walls, and obstacles — in God's time.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for breakthrough services or times of extended prayer.
arrangement_tips: Gospel production; choir and full band. Build to a climactic declaration.

title: All of That
summary_theology: Energetic testimony of gratitude for the fullness of what Christ accomplished — dying, rising, and reigning.
practical_worship_leadership: Very high energy; great for celebration services.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary gospel with heavy funk influence. Full band and choir.

title: Weary Traveler
summary_theology: Encourages the weary believer that rest is coming and the journey will end in the safety of God's presence.
practical_worship_leadership: Very effective for congregations in difficult seasons. Accessible and comforting.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary acoustic; warm and pastoral feel. Congregation receives it deeply.

title: The Blessing (feat. Kari Jobe)
summary_theology: Extended setting of the Aaronic blessing across multiple generations, declaring God's face shining on his people.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the most powerful songs of the COVID era; congregation receives it as a blessing.
arrangement_tips: Layers voices and instrumentation across 7+ minutes. Allow it to build organically.

title: Worthy of It All
summary_theology: Offers all of life as worthy of God because he is worthy of it all — total surrender as the proper response.
practical_worship_leadership: Meditative and deep; great for extended worship or altar moments.
arrangement_tips: Very sparse; allow the congregation to be still. Build slowly only if needed.

title: Goodness of God (Live)
summary_theology: CeCe Winans' gospel interpretation of the beloved Bethel song, adding rich gospel texture.
practical_worship_leadership: Works across gospel and contemporary contexts; congregation universally connects.
arrangement_tips: Gospel-influenced; choir adds depth. Allow extended soaking.

title: Ain't No Grave
summary_theology: Folk spiritual updated by Bethel declaring no grave can hold the body of the risen Lord or his people.
practical_worship_leadership: Extremely powerful for Easter. The congregation rises with the song.
arrangement_tips: Starts very spare; builds to massive full production. Allow silence before the first note.

title: Living Proof
summary_theology: Declares the transformed life as living proof of the grace and power of God's salvation.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for baptism, testimonial, or salvation-focus services.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary pop-rock; anthemic. Build to a strong final chorus.

title: Worthy
summary_theology: Simple, direct declaration of God's worthiness drawn from the heavenly worship of Revelation.
practical_worship_leadership: Excellent for soaking or extended worship. The simplicity allows deep focus.
arrangement_tips: Starts acoustic and builds gradually. Allow congregation to dwell in the declaration.

title: Proof of Your Love
summary_theology: Without love, every act of faith is empty noise; love is the proof that transforms words into worship.
practical_worship_leadership: Works well before a sermon on love or in a series on spiritual gifts.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary pop-rock; anthemic. Congregation easily engages.

title: Stand in Your Love
summary_theology: Declares the invincible security of standing in God's love against every fear and accusation.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for any service; congregation connects with the security language.
arrangement_tips: Full production; anthemic build. Very accessible for congregation.

title: Christ Be Magnified
summary_theology: Bold declaration that Christ will be magnified in every arena — whether the believer lives or dies.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful and bold; works as an anthem or a closing declaration.
arrangement_tips: Full production with building dynamics. The final chorus is very anthemic.

title: Seasons
summary_theology: Meditative trust in God through every season, finding the tree-like rootedness that bears fruit in all times.
practical_worship_leadership: Beautiful for transitions, new years, or any service on trust through change.
arrangement_tips: Gentle and reflective; piano-led. Do not over-produce.

title: Spirit Lead Me
summary_theology: Prayer for the Spirit to lead the believer even when understanding is absent, trusting his direction.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for any service on guidance, the Holy Spirit, or surrender.
arrangement_tips: Piano-led; builds gradually. Very congregational.

title: Gold
summary_theology: Declares trust in God's refining fire, believing the end result is gold worth the cost of the process.
practical_worship_leadership: Intimate and powerful; works for surrender moments or prayer ministry.
arrangement_tips: Sparse and soulful; keys and bass. Allow the congregation to be still.

title: Hold On to Me (Maverick City)
summary_theology: Honest prayer acknowledging weakness and holding onto the promise that God holds us even when we cannot hold on.
practical_worship_leadership: Effective for crisis contexts or services on anxiety and trust.
arrangement_tips: Soul-gospel feel; piano and gentle rhythm. Very intimate.

title: Jesus at the Center
summary_theology: Declares the commitment to keep Jesus at the absolute center of every area of life and ministry.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for any service; great for vision Sundays or missions focus.
arrangement_tips: Gospel-contemporary; build from sparse to full. Congregation sings the declaration.

title: I Will Follow
summary_theology: Simple, whole-hearted commitment to follow Jesus wherever he leads, in every circumstance.
practical_worship_leadership: Good for baptism, dedication, or a series on discipleship.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary rock; clean and accessible. Congregation joins the declaration.

title: Whom Shall I Fear (God of Angel Armies)
summary_theology: Declares God as the commander of angelic armies who fights for his people, removing every cause for fear.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for spiritual warfare services or any message on God's protection.
arrangement_tips: Full rock production; driving rhythm. Congregation sings the declaration boldly.

title: Same God
summary_theology: The God who parted seas and raised the dead is the same God here today — unchanging faithfulness.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for any service; congregation draws connection between biblical and personal history.
arrangement_tips: Build from very sparse to full band. The bridge is very powerful.

title: Talking to Jesus
summary_theology: Celebrates the simple practice of talking to Jesus as the foundation of spiritual life.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for a series on prayer. Accessible and relatable.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic and intimate; warm production. Very congregational.

title: Known
summary_theology: Declares the profound security of being fully known and fully loved by God at the same time.
practical_worship_leadership: Very accessible contemporary song; works for any service on identity.
arrangement_tips: Pop-contemporary; warm production. Congregation immediately resonates.

title: Hills and Valleys
summary_theology: Declares God as present and faithful in both the high hills of blessing and the low valleys of trial.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for any service; great for transitions or times of uncertainty.
arrangement_tips: Piano-led with gentle production. Very emotional and singable.

title: There Was Jesus
summary_theology: Personal testimony tracing every moment of crisis and joy — there was Jesus.
practical_worship_leadership: Very popular; congregation resonates with the testimony format.
arrangement_tips: Country-folk feel; acoustic guitar and piano. Warm and personal.

title: Forgiveness
summary_theology: Challenging song on the call to extend the same forgiveness received from Christ to those who have wronged us.
practical_worship_leadership: Convicting and powerful; works for a series on forgiveness or reconciliation.
arrangement_tips: Piano ballad; emotional. Allow congregation to sit with the challenge.

title: Hello My Name Is
summary_theology: Replacing the old labels of shame, addiction, and failure with the new name God gives in Christ.
practical_worship_leadership: Very powerful for recovery ministries or identity-focused services.
arrangement_tips: Pop-rock with emotional depth. Congregation declares the new name over themselves.

title: Is He Worthy
summary_theology: Uses the call-and-response of Revelation 5 to declare Christ worthy as the Lamb who was slain.
practical_worship_leadership: Very theologically rich; leader asks, congregation responds. Works very well.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic folk; call-and-response between leader and congregation. Very participatory.

title: We Will Feast in the House of Zion
summary_theology: The tension between present lament and future feast held together in the hope of Zion's banquet.
practical_worship_leadership: Excellent for justice-themed services or any service holding grief and hope together.
arrangement_tips: Folk-acoustic feel; simple and honest. Can be sung slowly and meditatively.

title: Psalm 23 (I Shall Not Want)
summary_theology: Contemporary folk setting of Psalm 23 that opens the ancient text to fresh congregational response.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for pastoral care services, funerals, or any service on God's provision.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic and gentle; allow the psalm text to carry the congregation.

title: King and a Kingdom
summary_theology: Challenges any conflation of the kingdom of God with political kingdoms, calling for undivided allegiance.
practical_worship_leadership: Prophetically challenging; good for a message on dual citizenship.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic folk; intimate and challenging. Not a high-energy song.

title: Work Song
summary_theology: Celebrates ordinary work as a form of worship and service to God and neighbor.
practical_worship_leadership: Labor Day Sunday, vocational ministry, or a series on faith and work.
arrangement_tips: Folk-ensemble feel; community singing. Very accessible.

title: Christ the Lord Is Risen Today
summary_theology: Wesley's Easter hymn of triumphant resurrection, with repeated Alleluia declaring victory over death.
practical_worship_leadership: The great Easter morning hymn. Congregation stands and sings with joy.
arrangement_tips: Full organ and brass; Alleluia after each line. Very festive.

title: Arise My Soul Arise
summary_theology: Wesley's confidence in Christ's heavenly intercession that silences the accusation of sin.
practical_worship_leadership: Good for Hebrews sermon series or any service on assurance of salvation.
arrangement_tips: Traditional four-part; piano or organ. Very stately and confident.

title: Let Us Love and Sing and Wonder
summary_theology: Rich Newton hymn of amazed response to redeeming grace, calling for love, song, and wonder.
practical_worship_leadership: One of Newton's best hymns; rewards careful, congregational singing.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary setting is popular; or traditional. Both build beautifully.

title: And Can It Be
summary_theology: Wesley's personal testimony of amazement that Christ died for him specifically, leading to bold assurance.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the great hymns of all time. Contemporary and traditional settings both work.
arrangement_tips: Strong march feel; can do acoustic or full organ. The bold I approach the eternal throne line is powerful.

title: Hark the Herald Angels Sing
summary_theology: Wesley's great Incarnation carol declaring the identity of the newborn King — God and sinner reconciled.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the greatest Christmas hymns. Full of theology.
arrangement_tips: Full organ and brass; congregation stands. Very familiar and beloved.

title: Joy to the World
summary_theology: Actually Watts' paraphrase of Psalm 98, declaring the universal reign of Christ over all creation.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic Christmas opener; every congregation knows it.
arrangement_tips: Full organ and brass; can add festive percussion. Let heaven and nature sing!

title: The First Noel
summary_theology: Medieval carol narrating the Nativity story from shepherds and magi to the birth of the Savior.
practical_worship_leadership: Very familiar; works for any Christmas service.
arrangement_tips: Waltz feel; choir or congregation. Traditional organ or acoustic guitar both work.

title: Away in a Manger
summary_theology: Beloved children's Christmas carol picturing the manger scene and praying for Christ's nearness.
practical_worship_leadership: Universally beloved; perfect for family or children's Christmas services.
arrangement_tips: Simple waltz; solo child voice or light piano. Very tender.

title: Go Tell It on the Mountain
summary_theology: African-American Christmas spiritual calling believers to proclaim the birth of Jesus from the mountaintop.
practical_worship_leadership: Very lively and participatory; great for a joyful Christmas service.
arrangement_tips: Gospel feel; choir, piano, full band. Very accessible and energetic.

title: One Bread One Body
summary_theology: Catholic contemporary communion hymn declaring the unity of the church in one bread and one body.
practical_worship_leadership: Works across denominations for communion. Very accessible.
