arrangement_tips: Piano and gentle rhythm; warm and reflective. Congregation joins easily.

title: Gather Us In
summary_theology: Gathering hymn welcoming all people into the community of faith around the table of God.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic contemporary Catholic hymn; widely used ecumenically.
arrangement_tips: Piano and choir; bright and welcoming. Works as a processional.

title: You Are Mine
summary_theology: Sets Isaiah 43 as an intimate dialogue of God's claim on his beloved, declaring do not be afraid.
practical_worship_leadership: Very moving for healing services, grief support, or baptism.
arrangement_tips: Piano and gentle accompaniment; the dialogue between God and believer is powerful.

title: On Eagles Wings (Contemporary)
summary_theology: Psalm 91 made singable, declaring the shelter and uplift of God for those who dwell in his shadow.
practical_worship_leadership: Beloved for funerals and any service on God's protection.
arrangement_tips: Piano and soloist; choir can join on choruses. Very warm and comforting.

title: Greater Is He
summary_theology: Declares the superiority of Christ over every enemy, temptation, and trial based on 1 John 4:4.
practical_worship_leadership: Energetic gospel declaration; great for spiritual warfare services.
arrangement_tips: Gospel piano and choir; strong rhythmic feel. Build declaration through repetition.

title: God Did
summary_theology: Simple but profound testimony song declaring God did what only he could do.
practical_worship_leadership: Works after testimonies are shared; very participatory chorus.
arrangement_tips: Gospel soul; allow the congregation to testify through repetition.

title: Great Things
summary_theology: Celebrates the great things God has done in creation and salvation, calling for continuous praise.
practical_worship_leadership: Very popular recent song; congregation engages immediately.
arrangement_tips: Full rock production; anthemic. Very singable chorus.

title: House of the Lord
summary_theology: Celebrates the joy of gathering in the house of the Lord, bringing joy as an act of worship.
practical_worship_leadership: High energy opener; congregation immediately joins. One of Phil Wickham's biggest songs.
arrangement_tips: Full rock-gospel production; driving groove. Very celebratory.

title: Stand By Me (Spiritual Version)
summary_theology: The classic plea for God to stand by the believer when darkness falls and the waters rise.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for general worship; very familiar melody. Can be adapted for worship contexts.
arrangement_tips: Soul feel; smooth rhythm section. The familiarity invites immediate participation.

title: The Power of the Cross
summary_theology: Vivid narration of the cross from Gethsemane to resurrection, declaring the power of Christ's sacrifice.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the finest Good Friday hymns of the modern era.
arrangement_tips: Piano and orchestra; allow the text to move the congregation. Never hurry.

title: The Gospel Song
summary_theology: Concise and theologically dense gospel song covering substitution, justification, and faith.
practical_worship_leadership: Excellent for a gospel-centered service or a series on Romans.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary piano; clear and clean. The theology should be heard clearly.

title: Grace Unmeasured
summary_theology: Rich meditation on the immeasurable grace of God shown in adopting sinners as his beloved children.
practical_worship_leadership: Theologically beautiful; rewards repeated singing.
arrangement_tips: Piano and acoustic; three verses that deepen the grace theme progressively.

title: How Deep the Father's Love
summary_theology: Contemplates the Father's costly love in giving the Son, and the Son's voluntary sacrifice for the unworthy.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the most theologically rich modern songs. Good Friday or communion.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic only — the simplicity is essential. Allow long pauses between verses.

title: We Are Standing on Holy Ground
summary_theology: Declares the gathered worshipers to be standing on holy ground in God's presence.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic for solemn moments of God's presence. Very widely known.
arrangement_tips: Piano and choir; soft and reverent. The congregation knows this immediately.

title: Lord Prepare Me (Sanctuary)
summary_theology: Prayer for God to make the believer a sanctuary, holy and pure, for his Spirit to dwell within.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the most widely sung simple worship songs. Works for any context.
arrangement_tips: Very simple; piano or acoustic guitar. Can be sung repeatedly as a prayer.

title: Shout to the North
summary_theology: Calls all nations and all people — men, women, the church — to shout praise from every direction.
practical_worship_leadership: Very energetic and inclusive. Works for missions or any celebration service.
arrangement_tips: Rock anthem; full band. The congregation responds very physically.

title: Did You Feel the Mountains Tremble
summary_theology: Envisions praise so powerful it shakes the mountains and causes all of creation to respond.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic Delirious anthem; congregation knows it. Great for high-energy worship.
arrangement_tips: Rock production; build from verse to massive chorus. Very familiar.

title: History Maker
summary_theology: Declaration of the desire to be a history-maker, used by God to change the world for his glory.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for missions or youth services. Very bold declaration.
arrangement_tips: Rock feel; driving energy. Congregation engages enthusiastically.

title: I Could Sing of Your Love Forever
summary_theology: Simple expression of unending devotion to the God whose love overwhelms and sustains.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic worship song from the 90s revival; still very effective.
arrangement_tips: Piano or acoustic guitar; can build to full band. Very accessible.

title: Never Once
summary_theology: Testimonial declaration that God has never once turned away or forsaken his people.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for testimonial services or any service on God's constant faithfulness.
arrangement_tips: Build carefully; start sparse, finish full. Very emotional and worshipful.

title: You Alone Can Rescue
summary_theology: Declares the uniqueness of Christ as the only rescuer, the only one who could bridge the divide to God.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for evangelistic services or any service on the uniqueness of Christ.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic build; the you alone can rescue counterpoint is very powerful.

title: Happy Day
summary_theology: Joyful celebration of salvation and resurrection, connecting to the old Philip Doddridge hymn.
practical_worship_leadership: Very high energy; great for Easter or a salvation-celebration service.
arrangement_tips: Rock production; full and joyful. The happy day chorus is very accessible.

title: Marvelous Light
summary_theology: Celebrates the marvelous light into which the believer has been called out of darkness.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for Easter or a series on identity and calling.
arrangement_tips: Rock production; anthemic. Very popular in early 2000s and still effective.

title: Glorious
summary_theology: Simple declaration of God's glory and the congregation's response of praise.
practical_worship_leadership: Very accessible; congregation picks it up immediately. Works as an opener.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary feel; piano and guitar. Clean and focused.

title: Because of Your Love
summary_theology: Celebrates the love of God as the foundation and motivation for all worship.
practical_worship_leadership: Gentle and accessible; works for most worship contexts.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary acoustic; warm and inviting. Good for opening or mid-service.

title: Graves Into Gardens
summary_theology: Declares God turns graves into gardens — impossibilities become testimonies of his resurrection power.
practical_worship_leadership: Very popular; congregation responds to the hope-filled imagery.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary production; builds steadily. Very anthemic bridge.

title: Do It Again
summary_theology: Asks God to repeat his acts of faithfulness in the present moment based on what he has done before.
practical_worship_leadership: Very powerful for congregations in difficulty. The bridge sustains extended worship.
arrangement_tips: Build from sparse to full production. The building tension releases powerfully.

title: Promises (Studio)
summary_theology: Rests in the settled assurance that God's promises are reliable and sufficient for every need.
practical_worship_leadership: Deep worship; allows extended soaking and prayer.
arrangement_tips: Neo-soul keys and percussion; allow space. Very groove-based.

title: Refiner
summary_theology: Prayer of surrender to the refining fire of God, trusting the process leads to pure gold.
practical_worship_leadership: Very intimate; works for surrender moments or prayer ministry.
arrangement_tips: Sparse and atmospheric; minimal production. Allow the congregation to be still.

title: My Worth Is Not in What I Own
summary_theology: One of the finest recent hymns on identity, rejecting every false measure of worth for Christ alone.
practical_worship_leadership: Very popular and powerful; congregation responds deeply.
arrangement_tips: Piano and guitar; three strong verses. Build gradually to the triumphant bridge.

title: Come Behold the Wondrous Mystery
summary_theology: Four-verse meditation on the mystery of the gospel — Incarnation, substitution, resurrection, and glorification.
practical_worship_leadership: Theologically rich new hymn; rewards congregational familiarity.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary acoustic; four beautiful verses. The mystery is best felt in a quiet arrangement.

title: Lord from Sorrows Deep
summary_theology: Honest lament hymn that brings deep sorrow to God while anchoring hope in Christ's faithfulness.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for grief, lament, or Good Friday services.
arrangement_tips: Piano and strings; very restrained. Allow the grief to be present without rushing to resolution.

title: Not to Us
summary_theology: Declares all glory to God alone, not to the worshiper, reflecting Psalm 115.
practical_worship_leadership: Excellent for humility-focused services or a series on glorifying God.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary acoustic; builds to a declaration. Very singable.

title: His Mercy Is More
summary_theology: Declares the mercy of God is always more abundant than the worst of human sin — a modern hymn of assurance.
practical_worship_leadership: Very popular modern hymn; congregation responds to the mercy language.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic guitar and piano; folk-contemporary blend. Three beautiful verses.

title: I Belong to Jesus
summary_theology: Bold declaration of belonging entirely to Jesus — body, soul, and spirit purchased by his blood.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for services on identity or after a baptism service.
arrangement_tips: Gospel production; choir and full band. Declarative and joyful.

title: Let Praise Arise
summary_theology: Calls praise to arise from every heart as the right and joyful response to God's goodness.
practical_worship_leadership: Great opener or mid-service celebration song.
arrangement_tips: Full contemporary production; anthemic. Congregation joins the declaration.

title: Nothing Like Your Love
summary_theology: Declares there is nothing in all creation to compare with the love of God.
practical_worship_leadership: Accessible and singable; congregation resonates with the wonder.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary acoustic-electric; warm production.

title: O Worship the King
summary_theology: Majestic hymn celebrating God as King through creation — the Ancient of Days who veils his light in song.
practical_worship_leadership: Very strong opening hymn for formal or traditional worship contexts.
arrangement_tips: Full organ; march feel. Can add brass. Four-part choir is majestic.

title: Praise to the Holiest in the Height
summary_theology: Newman's great hymn tracing the theology of the Incarnation and redemption with poetic precision.
practical_worship_leadership: Rich theological content; works for special services or Lent.
arrangement_tips: Tune Gerontius or Richmond; full organ and choir. Very stately.

title: Eternal Light Eternal Light
summary_theology: Questions how sinful beings can stand in the eternal light of God's holiness — answered only through grace.
practical_worship_leadership: Profound theological content; works for a holiness or grace series.
arrangement_tips: Hymn tune; organ or piano. Congregation should sing carefully and reflectively.

title: To God Be the Glory
summary_theology: Joyful ascription of all glory to God for the great things he has done in creation and redemption.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic that many older congregations love. Works for any celebratory service.
arrangement_tips: March feel; piano or organ. Very accessible and joyful.

title: Blessed Assurance
summary_theology: Joyful declaration of the assurance of salvation, resting in foretastes of glory and perfect submission.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the most sung American hymns. Works for any context.
arrangement_tips: Lilting 9/8 feel; piano and congregation. Very familiar.

title: Draw Me Nearer
summary_theology: Prayer for ever-deeper nearness to God, moving from consecrating service to mystical communion.
practical_worship_leadership: Works as a personal devotional prayer song or for services on spiritual growth.
arrangement_tips: Piano march feel; congregation sings with longing. Allow it to be gentle.

title: The King Is Coming
summary_theology: Vivid portrayal of Christ's return drawing the faithful home, calling believers to readiness and joyful anticipation of final redemption.
practical_worship_leadership: Building narrative song. Read the lyric story before singing to prime the congregation. Ends in triumphant declaration.
arrangement_tips: Choir-friendly. Builds from quiet anticipation to full orchestral finish. Brass and timpani on final section.

title: Something Beautiful
summary_theology: When believers surrender their broken lives to God, He exchanges them for something beautiful and meaningful beyond human imagining.
practical_worship_leadership: Tender invitation hymn. Use at altar calls or moments of surrender. The waltz feel creates gentle forward motion.
arrangement_tips: Piano and strings. Gentle dynamics throughout. Choir optional on final verse. Meditative pace.

title: Loving God, Loving Each Other
summary_theology: The two great commandments distilled: love God with everything and love one another, the twin pillars of Christian community and witness.
practical_worship_leadership: Joyful family song. Great for all-age services. Congregation picks it up quickly. Use for fellowship Sundays.
arrangement_tips: Upbeat, acoustic feel. Clapping optional. Works with piano/guitar duo or full band.

title: Oh Happy Day
summary_theology: The day of salvation is the happiest day of the believers life, when Jesus washed their sins away and they committed their lives to Him.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic gospel anthem. Choir-driven, full energy. Works as an opener or salvation response song.
arrangement_tips: Gospel piano, Hammond organ, choir. Call-and-response between lead and choir. High energy from start.

title: Changed
summary_theology: Through encounter with Christ, the believer is fundamentally changed from the inside out, bearing witness that God transforms those who surrender to Him.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful testimony ballad. Allow space for personal reflection. Good for stewardship or sanctification seasons.
arrangement_tips: Gospel piano-driven. Builds from intimate verse to powerful chorus with choir. Solo lead essential.

title: We Walk by Faith
summary_theology: The Christian life is one of trusting what is not yet seen, walking in confident reliance on God's promises even through difficulty.
practical_worship_leadership: Choir anthem. High energy, great for services on faith. Can be used as a call to perseverance.
arrangement_tips: Full choir with gospel band. Build dynamics through verses. Choir harmonies shine on chorus.

title: Hallelujah (Praise Jehovah)
summary_theology: A call to universal, whole-life praise of Jehovah, drawing on the great hallelujah tradition of the Psalter and culminating praise of Revelation.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic congregational praise hymn. Easy to teach, high participation. Great for opening worship.
arrangement_tips: Organ and choir traditional setting. Or bright piano/guitar for contemporary feel. March tempo keeps energy.

title: Victor
summary_theology: In Christ, the believer is more than a conqueror, declared victor over sin, death, and every form of spiritual opposition.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy gospel choir anthem. Excellent for breakthrough services. Congregation engages quickly.
arrangement_tips: Full choir, Hammond organ, gospel piano. Strong drum groove. Repeat chorus for congregational buildup.

title: You Are Good
summary_theology: Declaration of God's unchanging goodness as the foundation of worship, rooted in the Hebrew tradition of praising Yahweh for His steadfast character.
practical_worship_leadership: Energetic Messianic-style worship. Works in any contemporary setting. Congregation picks up chorus quickly.
arrangement_tips: Israeli folk feel with contemporary band. Guitar and percussion led. Repeat chorus builds momentum.

title: Kadosh (Holy)
summary_theology: The Hebrew Kadosh declares the absolute holiness of God, echoing the angelic cry before the throne of the Holy One of Israel.
practical_worship_leadership: Deep reverent worship. Use during times of consecration or extended worship sets. Hebrew word teaches congregation.
arrangement_tips: Minor modal feel, sparse instrumentation. Shofar optional. Builds into full band on repeated sections.

title: Whom Shall I Fear
summary_theology: If God is for the believer, there is no adversary to fear; the Lord is strength, light, and salvation, rendering all opposition powerless.
practical_worship_leadership: Meditative, confidence-building. Use in anxiety-heavy seasons or for persecuted church emphasis. Builds gently.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic guitar and piano led. Add strings for warmth. Congregation can join on repeated chorus from second time.

title: Bless the Lord (Ubarikiwe Bwana)
summary_theology: The Swahili worship tradition calls every part of the believer to bless and magnify God, expressing communal African praise with joyful abandon.
practical_worship_leadership: Participatory, movement-friendly. Works well in multicultural settings or mission contexts. Teach chorus phonetically.
arrangement_tips: Djembe and percussion led. Guitar and bass. Call-and-response structure. Communal dancing encouraged.

title: Rejoice (Jabulani)
summary_theology: Joy in the Lord expressed through the exuberant Zulu praise tradition, declaring that every day the Lord has made is reason for celebration.
practical_worship_leadership: High energy, celebratory. Use as a service opener or response to good news in the congregation. Natural movement.
arrangement_tips: African percussion, full band. Choir or congregation responds in call-and-response. Builds to joyful climax.

title: Worthy of It All
summary_theology: God alone is worthy of every sacrifice and surrender; all earthly gain counts as loss compared to knowing Christ and giving Him everything.
practical_worship_leadership: Meditative, extended worship flow song. Allow long instrumental sections. Soaking worship environment.
arrangement_tips: Keys-led, gentle build. Pads underneath. Guitar adds texture. Long outro for spontaneous worship.

title: Mountains
summary_theology: Faith declared with confidence that the God who made the mountains can move every obstacle; nothing is too hard for the One who holds all things.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful congregational anthem. Works for breakthrough moments or perseverance-focused services. Big sing.
arrangement_tips: Full band with keys prominent. Building dynamic through verse to chorus. Choir optional on bridge. Anthem ending.

title: Good God Almighty
summary_theology: Exuberant declaration of God's goodness across all of life, drawing the gathered community into joyful, whole-hearted praise of the Almighty.
practical_worship_leadership: Accessible, high-energy. Great opener. Congregation learns quickly. Works across all age groups.
arrangement_tips: Electric guitar-led with full band. Handclaps from the top. Short and repeatable chorus. Strong groove.

title: Holy Spirit Come
summary_theology: An earnest invitation for the Holy Spirit to move in power and presence, surrendering control to the sovereign work of God in gathered worship.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for transitioning into extended worship or altar time. Allow space for the Spirit to move. Avoid rushing.
arrangement_tips: Piano and pads. Add soft electric guitar. Leave room for silence and spontaneous prayer. Spacious arrangement.

title: Faith
summary_theology: Bold declaration of unwavering faith in God's word and character, choosing trust over circumstance and standing on the promises of Christ.
practical_worship_leadership: Declarations of faith work well in seasons of trial. Use after a faith-building message. Congregation empowered.
arrangement_tips: Piano-led gospel-influenced contemporary. Rich harmonies. Builds to triumphant declaration. Strong bridge.

title: Rock of Ages
summary_theology: The only hope of the sinner is the cleft rock of Christ crucified; no human effort or righteousness can avail, only the blood and water from His side.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic atonement hymn. Excellent for Good Friday or any cross-centered service. Deep theological weight.
arrangement_tips: Organ or piano, full congregation. Sing all four verses for full theological arc. Slow, reverent tempo.

title: Nearer, My God, to Thee
summary_theology: Even in darkness and hardship, the believer's desire and prayer is to draw nearer to God, finding in every trial a stairway toward heaven.
practical_worship_leadership: Tender, contemplative. Works for bereavement services or solemn seasons. Congregation knows this well.
arrangement_tips: Piano or organ. Gentle waltz feel. Harmonies on repeated lines. Can be arranged for strings quartet.

title: Leaning on the Everlasting Arms
summary_theology: Perfect fellowship and safety is found in leaning wholly on the everlasting arms of God; this is the source of the believer's joy and security.
practical_worship_leadership: Joyful, gentle hymn with a rocking rhythm. Works well in rural or traditional settings. Congregation loves it.
arrangement_tips: Piano with light swing feel. Banjo or acoustic guitar adds character. All voices together, no parts required.

title: Fairest Lord Jesus
summary_theology: Christ surpasses all earthly beauty — fairer than all the beauties of nature and heaven — and is the delight and glory of the worshiping heart.
practical_worship_leadership: Meditative adoration hymn. Works for quiet times of devotion or as a preparation-for-communion song.
arrangement_tips: Organ or piano. Gentle, unhurried tempo. Four-part harmony beautiful a cappella. Lush strings in contemporary arrangement.

title: Sweet By and By
summary_theology: The beautiful land on the far-off heavenly shore awaits all believers; this life of trial is temporary, and a glorious reunion with God is certain.
practical_worship_leadership: Comforting hope song. Use for memorial services, seniors ministries, or any focus on eternal hope.
arrangement_tips: Piano or organ. Gentle waltz. Can be arranged for male quartet or choir. Keep tempo unhurried.

title: When We All Get to Heaven
summary_theology: The brightest anticipation of the believer is the heavenly reunion when all the redeemed gather before the Lamb; this hope sustains and energizes earthly pilgrimage.
practical_worship_leadership: Joyful, triumphant. Great for closing services or any celebration of eternal hope. Congregation loves this.
arrangement_tips: Upbeat piano or organ. March feel. Full congregation from first verse. Optional brass on final verse.

title: In the Garden
summary_theology: The intimate walk and talk with the risen Lord in the garden captures the devotional heart of Christian faith: personal, transforming communion with the living Christ.
practical_worship_leadership: Beloved personal devotion hymn. Works for intimate services, seniors groups, or quiet contemplation moments.
arrangement_tips: Piano or organ, gentle waltz. Solo voice on verses is effective. Congregation joins chorus. Keep intimate atmosphere.

title: O Little Town of Bethlehem
summary_theology: In the still darkness of Bethlehem, the everlasting light of God was born, bringing hope and peace to those who welcome the silent wonder of Christ's coming.
practical_worship_leadership: Contemplative Christmas carol. Works for Advent candlelight or Christmas Eve services. Congregation knows it well.
arrangement_tips: Organ or piano. Gentle and unhurried. Harmonies on second verse. Strings or choir for Christmas Eve grandeur.

title: Away in a Manger
summary_theology: The sovereign Lord of heaven laid in a humble manger; this scene of holy poverty is the center of Christmas wonder, where God meets humanity in vulnerable love.
practical_worship_leadership: Essential for children's Christmas services. All ages know it. Can be done entirely by children's choir.
arrangement_tips: Piano gentle waltz. Can be unaccompanied for children. Simple, no complex harmonies needed.

title: We Three Kings
summary_theology: The visit of the Magi from the East symbolizes Christ as King, Priest, and Sacrifice, with the three gifts prefiguring His royal, priestly, and atoning offices.
practical_worship_leadership: Excellent for Epiphany Sunday. Each verse focuses a different gift. Minor key gives it dramatic weight.
arrangement_tips: Organ or brass. Minor feel works well with lower male voices. Congregation sings chorus; soloists on verses.

title: Even So Come
summary_theology: The ancient prayer Maranatha — Come Lord Jesus — expressed as a contemporary longing for the full consummation of God's kingdom and Christ's return.
practical_worship_leadership: Deeply moving, eschatological. Use in Advent or for focused prayer services. Creates holy longing in congregation.
arrangement_tips: Keys-led with pads. Building arrangement. Full band on chorus. Long bridge works for extended worship.

title: Church Arise
summary_theology: A rallying cry for the church to rise to its calling, awakening to the mission of God and standing together as the unified body of Christ in the world.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy anthem. Great for mission Sunday, church vision services, or summer conference contexts.
arrangement_tips: Guitar-driven rock feel. Full band, energetic. Choir adds body. Strong, punchy arrangement.

title: Thank You Jesus for the Blood
summary_theology: Heartfelt gratitude for the cleansing, redeeming blood of Jesus that breaks every chain and gives the believer boldness to stand before God.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful, emotional. Works well in communion services or consecration times. Congregation engages with personal gratitude.
arrangement_tips: Piano and keys. Vocal-driven, space for dynamics. Build through verses. Powerful bridge with declaration.

title: Survivor
summary_theology: Testimony of surviving spiritual darkness through God's grace, declaring that what was meant for destruction became a platform for God's glory.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy testimony anthem. Connects with those who have walked through difficulty. Congregation sings with conviction.
arrangement_tips: Southern rock feel. Electric guitar driven, full band. Punchy rhythm section. Congregation lifts voices naturally.

title: Truth Be Told
summary_theology: Calling Christians to authentic confession before God instead of performing okayness; the gospel is for honest sinners who need the truth of grace.
practical_worship_leadership: Disarming, honest. Creates space for real response. Use for confession/repentance moments or vulnerability-focused services.
arrangement_tips: Simple piano and acoustic guitar. Do not overdrive. The intimacy of the arrangement mirrors the lyric honesty.

title: Maybe It's OK
summary_theology: The gospel invitation to bring all of one's broken, imperfect self before God, finding that grace is not earned by performance but given freely to the weak.
practical_worship_leadership: Highly relatable, disarming. Use with honest, confessional services. Young adults connect strongly.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic-first arrangement. Do not over-produce. The rawness of the track mirrors the message. Gentle build.

title: Great Are You Lord
summary_theology: God breathes His life into all creation; worship rises as the natural response to the greatness of the God who sustains all things by His breath.
practical_worship_leadership: Beautiful 6/8 feel, unhurried. Creates deep atmosphere for adoration. Great for intimate or reflective services.
arrangement_tips: Piano and guitar, gentle. The 6/8 lilt creates natural movement. Build on bridge. Ethereal production qualities work well.

title: All I Have Is Christ
summary_theology: Personal testimony of counting all else as loss and finding in Christ alone the only treasure worth having, glorying in the cross above all worldly gain.
practical_worship_leadership: Compelling narrative testimony hymn. Use as an altar call response or for ordination services. Deeply moving.
arrangement_tips: Simple piano and guitar. Congregation learns quickly. Allow the melody to carry the lyric without over-arrangement.

title: Christ Is Mine Forevermore
summary_theology: Nothing in all creation can separate the believer from Christ; this security is the source of inexhaustible joy and peace in every circumstance.
practical_worship_leadership: Theologically grounding, emotionally warm. Works for assurance-focused services or for times of anxiety.
arrangement_tips: Piano-led. Organic, not over-produced. Four-part harmony works beautifully. Use acoustic band for intimacy.

title: Hosanna (Praise Is Rising)
summary_theology: Hosanna is both praise and a plea — save now — as the congregation joins in the ancient declaration welcoming the King who heals and restores.
practical_worship_leadership: Palm Sunday essential. Also works as a general praise opener. Congregation knows it. Joyful and accessible.
arrangement_tips: Full band, joyful feel. Verse builds toward chorus. Can do key change for final chorus. Simple and effective.

title: Today Is the Day
summary_theology: Each new day is the Lord's, a reason for rejoicing; the believer chooses to trust in God and rejoice today, laying worries aside for the joy set before them.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy opener or service response. Works for any celebration or kickoff. Very singable and joyful.
arrangement_tips: Guitar-driven contemporary rock feel. Full band, strong groove. Big bridge declaration. Clapping welcome.

title: Who You Say I Am
summary_theology: The believer's true identity is established not by past or performance but by the declaration of God: free, child, and chosen — fully known and fully loved.
practical_worship_leadership: Deeply impactful identity declaration. Works across all ages, especially youth and young adults. Personal response common.
arrangement_tips: Piano and keys led. Gentle build through verses to powerful chorus. Long bridge creates space for personal response.

title: Rattle!
summary_theology: The same power that raised dry bones to life and Christ from the dead is alive in the church; the resurrection power that rattled the grave energizes all worship.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy Easter anthem. Use for celebration moments or resurrection-focused services. The groove is infectious.
arrangement_tips: Full band, high energy. Drum-driven groove. Electric guitar leads. Works for big celebration moments.

title: Man of Sorrows
summary_theology: Christ the Man of Sorrows bore the full weight of human sin and grief; in taking our place under condemnation He brought substitutionary atonement and life.
practical_worship_leadership: Deeply moving for Good Friday or any cross-centered service. Congregation engages with weight of atonement truth.
arrangement_tips: Piano-led, restrained arrangement. Verses build in texture. Fourth verse shifts to declaration. Very powerful.

title: Raise a Hallelujah
summary_theology: Hallelujah is the weapon of the believer in every dark valley; raising praise in the face of opposition is an act of faith that testifies to God's certain victory.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for breakthrough moments or hospital/crisis contexts where song was written. Congregation responds with tenacity.
arrangement_tips: Piano and guitar. Building anthem. Full band on chorus. Repeat with increasing conviction.

title: Boldly I Approach
summary_theology: Through the blood of Christ the believer approaches the throne with boldness and confidence, no longer condemned but clothed in grace and welcomed as a child.
practical_worship_leadership: Joyful, confident. Great for services on grace or justification. Folk-rock energy delights congregations.
arrangement_tips: Banjo, acoustic guitar, percussion. Clapping from the top. Full-throated congregational singing. Joyful and rowdy.

title: Every Giant Will Fall
summary_theology: Just as the giant fell before David trusting in the name of the Lord, every obstacle in the believer's path falls before the sovereign power of God.
practical_worship_leadership: Energetic, faith-declaring. Great for breakthrough services or before a congregation-wide challenge. Kids love it too.
arrangement_tips: Guitar-driven folk-rock. Stomp and clap rhythm. Sing-along chorus. Bridge escalates intensity well.

title: Your Great Name
summary_theology: The name of Jesus carries unique power over sickness, sin, and every spiritual opposition; in that name alone is the full range of salvation and deliverance.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for healing services or deliverance contexts. Very singable. Use in extended worship or altar ministry.
arrangement_tips: Gospel piano and contemporary band. Build from quiet to powerful. Congregation joins naturally on chorus.

title: Talking to Jesus
summary_theology: Prayer is not a duty but a conversation with a living Friend; believers are invited into the intimacy of ongoing communion with Jesus, the one who always listens.
practical_worship_leadership: Creates a prayer-focused environment. Use to transition into a time of corporate intercession or personal prayer.
arrangement_tips: Gentle R&B/soul feel. Piano and light percussion. Intimate vocal delivery. Allow the groove to breathe.

title: Jireh
summary_theology: God is Jehovah Jireh, the One who provides; the believer learns contentment trusting that God is enough even when circumstances say otherwise.
practical_worship_leadership: Deeply moving declaration. Works for any service on provision, anxiety, or trusting God. Very personal and congregational.
arrangement_tips: Spacious, slow build. Piano-led. Allow declarations to breathe. Long outro for soaking. Multiple lead vocalists ideal.

title: Sublime Gracia (Amazing Grace)
summary_theology: The beloved testimony of grace sung in Spanish, carrying the power of the original in the language of millions of Spanish-speaking worshipers worldwide.
practical_worship_leadership: Excellent for multicultural services. Teach both Spanish and English. Congregation finds it accessible across languages.
arrangement_tips: Simple piano or guitar. Congregation likely knows both languages. Can alternate languages by verse.

title: Dios Es Amor
summary_theology: The foundational declaration of the Christian faith: God is love. The Argentinian worship tradition brings exuberant celebration of this eternal truth.
practical_worship_leadership: Joyful Latin rhythm. Works in multicultural congregations. Teach congregation a few Spanish phrases to sing along.
arrangement_tips: Latin rhythmic feel with contemporary band. Guitar-driven. Percussion adds authentic Latin character.

title: En Tu Nombre (In Your Name)
summary_theology: Everything done in the name of Jesus carries His authority; the believer surrenders all activity and worship under the lordship of the name above all names.
practical_worship_leadership: Bilingual worship bridge possibility. Christine D'Clario connects with Spanish and English congregations equally.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary sound with Latin influence. Piano and guitar. Can lead in Spanish or English or both.

title: You Are Holy (Geokdashi Georockdashi)
summary_theology: The Korean church's tradition of fervent, heart-deep worship expressed in declarations of God's holiness that come from a culture of deep reverence and sacrifice.
practical_worship_leadership: Works in multicultural settings. Teaching phonetically in Korean is impactful. Korean congregations respond immediately.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary Korean worship style. Piano and full band. Emotional expressiveness valued in performance.

title: Heart of Worship
summary_theology: True worship strips away performance and style to bring the simple gift of a surrendered heart; it is all about Jesus and nothing else.
practical_worship_leadership: Seminal worship song for teaching the meaning of worship. Use when congregation needs to refocus on why they worship.
arrangement_tips: Very simple arrangement. Acoustic guitar or piano. The point is simplicity. Do not over-produce this song.

title: Blessed Be Your Name
summary_theology: The name of the Lord is blessed both in abundance and in suffering; this radical declaration of praise regardless of circumstance echoes Job and the prophets.
practical_worship_leadership: Congregation knows it well. Works especially in difficult seasons. The declaration of praise in hardship is deeply powerful.
arrangement_tips: Full band, energetic but with emotional weight. Verses tell both sides — blessing and trial. Chorus is the declaration.

title: Rain Down
summary_theology: Crying out for the latter rain of the Spirit to fall afresh, reviving dry hearts and bringing new seasons of spiritual harvest and awakening.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for revival or renewal emphasis. Works for extended worship and prayer meetings. Build toward passionate cry.
arrangement_tips: Piano and guitars, building arrangement. Dynamics are key. Intimate pleading verses, full-band chorus.

title: My Obsession
summary_theology: Full, consuming devotion to Christ above all other loves and pursuits; the believer whose gaze is fixed on God finds that nothing else compares.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy, passionate declaration. Works well for younger congregations or mission/youth contexts.
arrangement_tips: Rock-driven, full band. Guitar-led with strong drums. Build to chorus. Energy carries congregation forward.

title: O Praise Him
summary_theology: All creation is invited to turn its gaze and voice toward God in extravagant praise; the worshiper joins the cosmic chorus of adoration.
practical_worship_leadership: Joyful, upbeat. Great opener or response song. Works across generations. The playful musical feel delights congregations.
arrangement_tips: Banjo, acoustic guitar, full band. Dance rhythm. Congregation moves naturally. Short and repeatable.

title: How He Loves
summary_theology: God's love is extravagant, jealous, and overwhelming; it is the kind of love that does not abandon in grief but rushes in with healing presence.
practical_worship_leadership: Deeply moving, emotionally intense. Use for times of grief, healing, or simply celebrating God's love. Allow response time.
arrangement_tips: Simple piano, building gently. Full band on chorus. Bridge is emotionally powerful. Congregation often weeps.

title: Love Is Stronger Than the Grave
summary_theology: The love of God in Christ is stronger than death, the grave, and every power of darkness; the resurrection proves that love has the final word.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for Easter, memorial services, or grief ministry. Congregation holds the declaration with conviction.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary Southern Gospel. Strong piano and band. Build from intimate to powerful. Choir optional.

title: Take Heart
summary_theology: The words of Jesus before the crucifixion: take heart, I have overcome the world. His peace is a gift not dependent on circumstances but on His victory.
practical_worship_leadership: Meditative, comforting. Use in services addressing fear, difficulty, or uncertainty. Creates a place of rest for the congregation.
arrangement_tips: Piano-led, spacious. Pads and ambient guitar add atmosphere. Very unhurried, contemplative pace.

title: Abundance
summary_theology: God is not a God of scarcity but of overflowing abundance; the believer's cup runs over as they encounter the extravagant generosity of the Father.
practical_worship_leadership: Good for stewardship, blessing, or gratitude seasons. Creates an atmosphere of open-handed receiving.
arrangement_tips: R&B-influenced contemporary worship. Piano and groove. Allow the song to breathe and flow.

title: Victory
summary_theology: Christ has disarmed the powers of darkness and led captivity captive; the believer stands in total victory through the finished work of the cross and empty tomb.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy celebration anthem. Great for breakthrough moments or Easter. Congregation responds with full energy.
arrangement_tips: Full band, guitar and keys driven. Strong rhythm. Repeat chorus and bridge multiple times. Triumphant feel.

title: King of Wonders
summary_theology: The wonders of creation point to the glory of their Creator; the God who set the stars in place deserves the fullness of worship from all He has made.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy, wonder-inspiring. Works as a service opener or after a creation-focused message. Great for outdoor or summer services.
arrangement_tips: Full band, anthemic feel. Guitar and keys prominent. Building arrangement to big final chorus.

title: Communion
summary_theology: The communion table is where the believer remembers the broken body and poured-out blood of Christ; each celebration is a proclamation of the cross until He returns.
practical_worship_leadership: Excellent for communion services. Simple and accessible. Congregation enters naturally into the remembrance.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic feel. Simple piano or guitar. The simplicity serves the sacrament. No need for complex arrangement.

title: Hope of the Nations
summary_theology: Christ is the hope not only of individual believers but of all nations; the full extent of the gospel reaches to every tribe, tongue, and people.
practical_worship_leadership: Mission-focused, global vision. Use for mission Sunday, world vision services, or global outreach emphasis.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic folk feel. Simple and singable. Works well in smaller gatherings or house church contexts.

title: Jesus Thank You
summary_theology: The response to the full scope of atonement — substitution, justification, and new life — is overflowing gratitude from hearts that understand what grace has done.
practical_worship_leadership: Excellent as a communion preparation song or Good Friday meditation. Rich theology clothed in beautiful melody.
arrangement_tips: Piano or guitar-led. Contemporary hymn approach. Gentle build. Irish folk influences in some arrangements.

title: In Christ Alone (I Stand)
summary_theology: The entirety of Christian hope, identity, and security is found in Christ alone; from birth through death and resurrection, His work is the only foundation that stands.
practical_worship_leadership: Perhaps the defining contemporary hymn of the early 21st century. Use for any foundational gospel-centered service.
arrangement_tips: All four verses tell the full gospel arc. Do not cut verses. Piano or guitar. Congregation knows it deeply.

title: The Chasing Song
summary_theology: An evangelistic song about running to God who is running to meet the returning prodigal; the invitation to come home resonates with any seeking heart.
practical_worship_leadership: Strong for evangelism services or altar call settings. Narrative and accessible. Non-believers connect to the prodigal frame.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic folk-pop. Energetic but warm. Guitar-driven. Full band on chorus. Works well with visual storytelling.

title: Wedding Dress
summary_theology: A prophetic challenge to the church that trades the purity of covenantal love with Christ for lesser pursuits; the bride is called back to faithfulness to her Bridegroom.
practical_worship_leadership: Prophetic, convicting. Works for a reformation or repentance emphasis. Best used with careful pastoral framing.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic folk. The prophet voice works better when unadorned. Simple, direct delivery. Let the lyric do the work.

title: Praise
summary_theology: Praise is both a declaration of who God is and a weapon against darkness; lifting His name in every circumstance is an act of defiant, victorious faith.
practical_worship_leadership: Very high energy, excellent for celebration or breakthrough services. The repeated declaration builds strong faith atmosphere.
arrangement_tips: Guitar-driven anthem. Full band, high energy throughout. Loop the chorus and bridge for extended worship.

title: Nobody Like You
summary_theology: There is nobody in all of creation like God; His love, power, and character are utterly unique, making worship the only fitting response from those who know Him.
practical_worship_leadership: Joyful, accessible. Great for services focusing on the uniqueness of Christ. Congregation picks it up quickly.
arrangement_tips: R&B-influenced groove. Piano and guitar. Hand claps and movement welcomed. Natural celebration energy.

title: God Given (For Your Glory)
summary_theology: All of life and breath was made by God for His glory; the believer offers everything back to the One from whom all good gifts come.
practical_worship_leadership: Good for stewardship services or full-surrender moments. Brooke Fraser's songwriting resonates across contemporary congregations.
arrangement_tips: Piano and full band. Clean, professional production. Build through song. Bridge works for extended worship.

title: Spirit Break Out
summary_theology: A passionate cry for the Holy Spirit to break through every barrier and move in sovereign revival power, consuming the church with holy fire.
practical_worship_leadership: Fervent revival prayer song. Works for extended corporate prayer or Holy Spirit emphasis. Creates space for surrender.
arrangement_tips: Building anthem. Start intimate, escalate. Full band on chorus. Long outro for extended prayer worship.

title: Worthy of Every Song
summary_theology: Joining the heavenly worship of the Lamb who is worthy of every song, honor, and praise; earth's worship is an anticipation of the eternal throne-room song.
practical_worship_leadership: Meditative, throne-room atmosphere. Use for deep corporate adoration. Not for rushed services — needs space to breathe.
arrangement_tips: Keys and pads led. Very spacious arrangement. Long sections of instrumental. Congregation engages in personal adoration.

title: Voice of God
summary_theology: The voice that spoke creation into existence still speaks to the hearts of His children; in His word and by His Spirit, God continues to address and guide His people.
practical_worship_leadership: Meditative, reverence-inducing. Works for scripture-reading worship sets or any focus on hearing from God.
arrangement_tips: Spacious, ethereal sound. Keys and pads. Subtle guitar. Allow silence between sections. Congregation engages quietly.

title: Stand Up for Jesus
summary_theology: The soldiers of Christ are called to stand firm under the banner of Christ, fighting the good fight of faith until the final victory is won.
practical_worship_leadership: March-like, commissioning energy. Use for missions, ordinations, or spiritual warfare emphasis services.
arrangement_tips: Organ or brass-led, march tempo. Full congregation. Can be modernized with full band arrangement.

title: At Calvary
summary_theology: Grateful testimony of what Calvary accomplished: years of folly and darkness were exchanged for grace and light through the atoning sacrifice of Christ.
practical_worship_leadership: Testimonial hymn, works for any cross-focused service. Congregation of traditional bent knows it; others learn quickly.
arrangement_tips: Waltz feel, piano or organ. All verses tell the full testimony arc. Gentle and grateful in spirit.

title: When Morning Gilds the Skies
summary_theology: Every morning and every activity is to be saturated with the name and praise of Jesus; the whole of daily life becomes an act of worship.
practical_worship_leadership: Beautiful morning worship hymn. Works for any service, especially Sunday morning. Congregation of traditional bent loves it.
arrangement_tips: Organ or piano. Four-part harmony is beautiful. Can be done a cappella. Simple and flowing melody.

title: Thou Art Worthy
summary_theology: Drawing directly from the throne-room vision of Revelation, this song declares God worthy of glory, honor, and power as Creator of all things.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic charismatic-era worship song. Reverent, direct Scripture. Works in any denominational context.
arrangement_tips: Simple piano or organ. Slow, reverent tempo. Congregation moves naturally into personal adoration as they sing.

title: Celebrate Jesus
summary_theology: The resurrection of Jesus Christ is the ultimate reason for celebration; because He lives, the church erupts in perpetual triumph and joy.
practical_worship_leadership: Short, high-energy, known everywhere. Great for Easter or any celebration moment. Easy to use as a repeated declaration.
arrangement_tips: Upbeat, full band or choir. Very short and easily repeatable. Works as a bookend to longer worship songs.

title: You Are My King (Amazing Love)
summary_theology: The amazing love of the King who laid down His crown and His life for sinners; the response to such sacrifice is worship, submission, and total devotion.
practical_worship_leadership: Widely known and loved. Works at Easter or any atonement-focused service. Congregation sings with full heart.
arrangement_tips: Piano or guitar led. Full band on chorus. Very clean arrangement works best. Congregation joins easily.

title: I Could Sing of Your Love Forever
summary_theology: The love of God in Christ is so overwhelming that it demands perpetual song; no finite expression of praise can exhaust the depth of what He has done.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic worship anthem known by virtually every church. Works anywhere, any style. Very accessible and participatory.
arrangement_tips: Simple arrangement. Congregation sings without prompting. Can do acoustic or full band. Works well a cappella.

title: Shout to the Lord
summary_theology: A full-throated shout of worship to the sovereign Lord who is the delight and strength of the believer; all creation joins in this declaration.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the most widely sung worship songs globally. Works everywhere. Congregation sings without hesitation.
arrangement_tips: Piano-led, full band. The key of Bb suits a wide vocal range. Allow the climax to build naturally.

title: Open the Eyes of My Heart
summary_theology: Prayer for the eyes of the heart to be opened to see the glory and holiness of God as Isaiah saw it; the believer desires to behold the Holy One.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic, known everywhere. Works as a prayer-beginning song or during transition into worship. Very accessible.
arrangement_tips: Guitar-driven, moderate energy. Full band or acoustic both work well. Congregation picks it up from the first chord.

title: Draw Me Close
summary_theology: A prayer of surrender and longing: drawing near to God, asking Him to fill every need, and releasing all else in exchange for intimate knowledge of Him.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic intimate worship song. Works for any age, any tradition. Creates a tender atmosphere for personal devotion.
arrangement_tips: Piano-led, very simple. Do not over-produce. The intimacy of the lyric calls for minimalist arrangement.

title: Seek Ye First
summary_theology: Setting kingdom priorities first, trusting that God provides all other needs; ask, seek, knock in faith that the Father who knows our needs will supply.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic, universally known. Works for scripture-based teaching or prayer emphasis. Very accessible across traditions.
arrangement_tips: Simple guitar or piano, gentle waltz. Rounds can be done with this song. All ages participate naturally.

title: Be Exalted, O God
summary_theology: The cry for God to be exalted above all the earth, calling for His glory to fill the heavens and His name to be praised among the nations.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic worship song with a Scripture-saturated lyric. Good for any tradition. Works well in quieter, meditative moments.
arrangement_tips: Piano or organ. Simple, unhurried. Works beautifully a cappella. Choir harmonies add depth.

title: As the Deer
summary_theology: As the deer pants for water, the believer pants for God; this deep thirst for the living God is satisfied only in intimate encounter with Him.
practical_worship_leadership: Universal classic. Everyone knows it across traditions. Works in any moment of quiet, seeking worship.
arrangement_tips: Piano or guitar, simple and gentle. The familiarity means congregation enters immediately. No complex arrangement needed.

title: We Bring the Sacrifice of Praise
summary_theology: Praise offered to God is a sacrifice — a deliberate, costly, joyful offering from those who bring it as an act of the will regardless of circumstance.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic charismatic worship song. Joyful, participatory. Works well at any point in service. Everyone knows it.
arrangement_tips: Upbeat, piano or guitar. Congregation sings from first note. Short, easily repeated. Clapping welcomed.

title: Bless His Holy Name
summary_theology: Direct Scripture turned into a song of total, whole-being praise; the soul and everything within it is called to bless the holy name of the Lord.
practical_worship_leadership: Short, Scripture-saturated. Works as a choral response or congregational call. Easy to insert anywhere in worship.
arrangement_tips: Gospel piano, choir response. Very short and repeatable. Works powerfully a cappella in choir settings.

title: Let's Just Praise the Lord
summary_theology: Simple, communal invitation to corporate praise; the gathered people of God lifting voices together in joyful celebration of all He has done.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic Gaither singalong. Southern Gospel tradition. Works for camp meetings, fellowships, or informal worship.
arrangement_tips: Piano and choir, joyful. Easy to sing. Works with simple acoustic arrangement or full Southern Gospel band.

title: Father, I Adore You
summary_theology: Trinitarian devotion expressed in simple, direct adoration of Father, Son, and Spirit; the whole Trinity is addressed in successive verses of surrender.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic three-verse Trinitarian song. Works a cappella in rounds. Meditative and universally accessible. All traditions embrace it.
arrangement_tips: A cappella or simple piano. Works in three-part round. Each verse addresses a person of the Trinity. Unhurried.

title: Give Thanks
summary_theology: Thanksgiving to God even in weakness, rooted in the paradox that human weakness becomes strength through Christ; the poor and weak are made rich and strong in Him.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic, universally known. Works for thanksgiving services, stewardship, or any theme of gratitude and grace.
arrangement_tips: Simple guitar or piano. Congregation sings from the first note. Very accessible, no complex arrangement needed.

title: Exalt the Lord Our God
summary_theology: Direct Scripture song from Psalm 99 calling the congregation to exalt the holy Lord God and worship at His footstool, for He is holy.
practical_worship_leadership: Scripture-saturated classic. Works at any point in worship. Easy for congregation. Focused on holiness of God.
arrangement_tips: Simple piano or guitar. Works well a cappella. Short and easily repeated. No complex arrangement needed.

title: Trading My Sorrows
summary_theology: The divine exchange: sorrows, shame, and sickness traded for joy and resurrection life; this is the substance of the atonement applied in daily experience.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy, celebratory. The Yes Lord section creates a great antiphonal moment. Works well for younger congregations.
arrangement_tips: Upbeat contemporary praise. Guitar-driven. The Yes Lord section is call-and-response. Full band, clapping welcome.

title: Worthy Is the Lamb
summary_theology: Joining the heavenly anthem: the Lamb who was slain is worthy of all power, riches, wisdom, strength, honor, glory, and blessing forever.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic Hillsong anthem. Works well as a closing worship song or after communion. Congregation sings with depth.
arrangement_tips: Piano-led, building. Full band on chorus. Long ending allows extended worship. Choir adds richness.

title: Let the River Flow
summary_theology: The prophetic river of the Spirit flowing from the throne of God brings life to everything it touches; the church prays for this river to flow in power.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic revival prayer song. Creates space for Holy Spirit ministry. Works for extended worship or prayer meetings.
arrangement_tips: Piano and guitar. Fluid, flowing arrangement style. Build gradually. Allow spontaneous singing during instrumental sections.

title: Whole Heart (Hold Me Now)
summary_theology: The prayer of the wholehearted believer: to hold fast to God and be held by Him, surrendering every part to the One whose love is the only true anchor.
practical_worship_leadership: Intimate, devotional. Works well for consecration or commitment moments. Congregation resonates with the holding-fast imagery.
arrangement_tips: Piano and pads. Warm acoustic guitar. Build gently. Allow space for personal response in the bridge.

title: Moving Forward
summary_theology: Pressing forward and not looking back, the believer trusts that the God of the new thing is doing something fresh; past failures and victories both yield to future hope.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for New Year, new season, or transition moments. Also powerful after a community difficulty. Forward-looking faith.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary gospel-influenced. Full band with energy. Build through chorus. Choir adds dimension.

title: Too Good to Not Believe
summary_theology: God is so consistently good and faithful that to doubt His power to work again is to deny the evidence of all He has already done; He is too good not to believe.
practical_worship_leadership: Reassuring for faith seasons. Good for healing or breakthrough services. Declaration that builds expectation.
arrangement_tips: Piano and guitar. Contemporary feel. Build through verses to full band chorus. Bridge is a faith declaration.

title: Yes and Amen
summary_theology: Every promise of God finds its yes in Christ; the believer stands on the unshakeable word of a God who cannot lie and does not change His mind.
practical_worship_leadership: Strong declaration of God's faithfulness. Works well for promise-focused or perseverance services. Bridge is very singable.
arrangement_tips: Full band with keys prominent. Build through song. Very congregational on chorus. Key change option for final section.

title: Real Love
summary_theology: The love God shows in sending Christ is the most real love the world has ever known; young worshipers are called to discover and live in this authentic, transforming love.
practical_worship_leadership: High energy, youth-oriented. Great for youth services or all-age contemporary services. Very accessible and upbeat.
arrangement_tips: Electronic elements, synth, and guitar. Dance energy. Youth respond immediately. Can use for upbeat opener.

title: Alive
summary_theology: Because Christ is alive, the believer is alive in Him; the resurrection power that raised Jesus from the dead now animates the life of all who are in Him.
practical_worship_leadership: Very high energy. Works for youth events, Easter, or any celebration of resurrection life. Young people love it.
arrangement_tips: Electronic production with full band. High BPM, dance feel. Works for big celebration moments or openers.

title: Fire Never Sleeps
summary_theology: The fire of the Holy Spirit in the believer never sleeps and never dies; this holy passion drives mission, prayer, and the ongoing pursuit of God.
practical_worship_leadership: Use for Holy Spirit emphasis or prayer meetings. Creates longing for more of God. Good for extended worship.
arrangement_tips: Indie worship feel. Guitar-driven, atmospheric production. Build through song. Allow spontaneous expression.

title: Love Came Down
summary_theology: God in all His fullness stepped into human flesh, bringing His love down to the broken and lost; the Incarnation is the definitive act of divine pursuit.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for Christmas, Advent, or any incarnation-focused service. The simple joy of the lyric resonates widely.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic and warm. Simple piano or guitar. Build to full band. The joy in the song should be reflected in the delivery.

title: Another in the Fire
summary_theology: As the fourth man walked with Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego in the fire, God promises His presence in every trial; the believer is never alone in the flames.
practical_worship_leadership: Deeply comforting in hard seasons. The Daniel narrative connects powerfully. Good for cancer, grief, or any trial context.
arrangement_tips: Guitar-driven, emotive. Build through the song. Long bridge works for extended prayer or response ministry.

title: Pieces
summary_theology: God receives every broken piece of the believer with patient love, gathering what is shattered and making it whole in His own way and time.
practical_worship_leadership: Very healing-focused. Works for people carrying shame, loss, or brokenness. Allow extended time for response.
arrangement_tips: Sparse piano and pads. Very intimate. Do not rush. Long instrumental sections for prayer and response.

title: Beautiful Things
summary_theology: God makes beautiful things out of dust and darkness; His redemptive power transforms what is broken, ugly, and lost into something glorious.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for redemption stories, testimony services, or any emphasis on new creation. Congregation loves the message.
arrangement_tips: Indie folk feel. Guitar and piano. Build through song. Great for outdoor or creative worship contexts.

title: Nothing Is Impossible
summary_theology: The declaration that nothing is impossible with God; His power reaches into every impossible situation and His word can accomplish whatever He purposes.
practical_worship_leadership: Faith-building, expectation-raising. Works before prayer for healing or any moment of believing for the miraculous.
arrangement_tips: Full contemporary band. High energy. Chorus is very singable. Bridge escalates conviction. Key change optional.

title: Follow You Anywhere
summary_theology: The wholehearted commitment to follow Jesus wherever He leads, surrendering personal plans and preferences for the sake of discipleship.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for discipleship or commitment series. Works for altar calls or renewal of commitment. Personal and corporate.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary, guitar-driven. Building arrangement. Bridge is a declaration of commitment. Congregation sings strongly.

title: Crown Him with Many Crowns
summary_theology: The many crowns of Christ — as Lamb, Lord of life, Lord of love, Lord of lords — are each celebrated in this coronation hymn of the King of kings.
practical_worship_leadership: Majestic, triumphant. Works for Ascension Sunday, Christ the King, or any celebration of His lordship. Congregation loves it.
arrangement_tips: Organ and brass, or contemporary full band. March feel. Sing all verses for full coronation narrative.

title: All Creatures of Our God and King
summary_theology: All of creation is summoned to praise the Creator; sun, moon, wind, and water all join the alleluia of those who love God and serve Him.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for creation care, Earth Day, or any nature-focused service. Also a general praise hymn. All ages sing it.
arrangement_tips: Organ or brass. March/processional feel. Alleluia refrain is the sing-along. Can use with a congregational procession.

title: O for a Thousand Tongues to Sing
summary_theology: Written on the anniversary of Wesley's conversion, this hymn bursts with the desire for more voices to declare the saving power and praises of the God of grace.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for any service focused on testimony or evangelism. Opening hymn of the Methodist hymnal. Very congregational.
arrangement_tips: Organ or piano. Robust congregation singing. Sing multiple verses for full theological arc. Can also do contemporary arrangement.

title: Come Thou Fount of Every Blessing
summary_theology: The prayer of a wandering heart to be captured and kept by the grace that has already done so much; the Ebenezer stone of remembrance anchors ongoing trust.
practical_worship_leadership: Deeply beloved hymn. Works for any service. Congregation often knows it by heart. The wandering-heart lyric resonates widely.
arrangement_tips: Piano or guitar, waltz feel. Contemporary arrangements abound. Congregation sings with great feeling. Do all three verses.

title: Joyful, Joyful We Adore Thee
summary_theology: Beethoven's Ode to Joy becomes a hymn of joyful adoration to the God of all creation; every creature is invited into the celestial symphony of praise.
practical_worship_leadership: Joyful, processional. Works for celebration services, Easter, Pentecost, or Christmas. Congregation sings with delight.
arrangement_tips: Organ and brass, or orchestra. March feel. Works with contemporary full band as well. Very accessible melodically.

title: Praise to the Lord, the Almighty
summary_theology: The sovereign Lord of creation who orders all things in wisdom and grace is worthy of the highest praise from every creature He has made.
practical_worship_leadership: Majestic German Reformed hymn. Works as a processional or opening hymn for any celebratory service.
arrangement_tips: Organ, brass, or full orchestra. 3/4 stately feel. Congregation knows it widely. Sing all four verses for full praise arc.

title: Nothing I Hold Onto
summary_theology: The believer holds nothing so tightly that it cannot be released to God; open-handed living is the pathway to receiving all that God has to give.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for any surrender moment. Very simple lyric with deep impact. Good for decision points and altar ministry.
arrangement_tips: Sparse acoustic. Very minimal. The space in the song is intentional. Allow silence for personal release.

title: God of This City
summary_theology: God is the God of every city and nation; His glory is greater than the darkness that fills urban spaces, and He is bringing His kingdom to transform them.
practical_worship_leadership: Excellent for urban ministries, missions services, or city-wide prayer gatherings. Congregation sings with missional conviction.
arrangement_tips: Full band, powerful. Build through verses. Chorus is anthemic. Works at large events or urban church contexts.

title: Furious
summary_theology: God's love is furious in its pursuit of wayward humanity — relentless, intense, and unstoppable; it is not passive or gentle but a consuming fire of divine affection.
practical_worship_leadership: Intense adoration song. Creates deep atmosphere. Use for extended worship or soaking contexts. Very personal and moving.
arrangement_tips: Piano-led with atmospheric guitar. Build slowly. Allow declarations to land. Long outro for personal response.

title: Surrounded (Fight My Battles)
summary_theology: When the enemy is all around, the believer is even more surrounded by the armies of God; praise is the weapon that routs the foe and brings breakthrough.
practical_worship_leadership: Very powerful for spiritual warfare or breakthrough contexts. Congregation takes up the declaration with conviction.
arrangement_tips: Building anthem. Start simple, build to full band. Repeat the key declaration. Room for extended worship in bridge.

title: Behold the Lamb of God
summary_theology: The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world is born, lives, dies, and rises; this extended narrative song traces the full arc of the incarnation and atonement.
practical_worship_leadership: Excellent for Advent-Christmas or communion. Works especially for storytelling worship sets. Invites meditation on the full gospel.
arrangement_tips: Piano or acoustic. Waltz feel. Allow each verse to build the narrative. Can be done as a full Christmas cantata.

title: Name Above All Names
summary_theology: The name of Jesus stands supreme above every other name; at this name every knee will bow and every tongue confess His absolute lordship.
practical_worship_leadership: Works as a bridge between worship sets or as a focused declaration time. Very accessible and powerful.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary full band. Piano and guitar prominent. Build through the song. Strong bridge is a declaration of triumph.

title: Kyrie Eleison (Lord Have Mercy)
summary_theology: The ancient prayer of mercy drawn from the Psalms and used in Christian liturgy from the earliest centuries; the humble cry of the sinner before a holy God.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for confession and absolution moments in liturgical worship. Can be chanted or sung in modern arrangement.
arrangement_tips: Meditative, sparse arrangement. Greek Kyrie Eleison works powerfully in a chanted setting. Modern arrangements also effective.

title: The Summons (Will You Come and Follow Me)
summary_theology: Five questions from Christ to the potential disciple; the invitation to follow regardless of cost and the promise of transformation through obedience.
practical_worship_leadership: Profound discipleship hymn. Works at ordination, baptism, confirmation, or any commitment service. Slowly builds resolve.
arrangement_tips: Gentle guitar or piano. Five verses that build in conviction. Congregation may answer the questions personally in silent prayer.

title: Gratitude
summary_theology: Offering gratitude to God not because circumstances are good but because He is good; choosing thanksgiving as an act of faith in every season.
practical_worship_leadership: Deeply personal. Works for any season. Congregation resonates with the choice to be grateful in pain. Very powerful.
arrangement_tips: Piano-led, intimate. Build through song. Bridge is especially powerful. Do not rush the dynamic build.

title: Child of Love
summary_theology: The identity-shaping truth that believers are loved children of God; this belonging is not earned but freely given in the adoption of grace.
practical_worship_leadership: Joyful identity song. Works for all ages. Youth and children respond strongly. Good for baptism or welcome services.
arrangement_tips: Folk-pop feel. Guitar and keys. Joyful energy throughout. Congregation sings naturally. Works for outdoor services.

title: God So Loved
summary_theology: The most famous verse in Scripture becomes a song: the width, length, height, and depth of God's love expressed in the gift of His Son for a broken world.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for evangelism services, Good Friday, or any service centered on John 3:16. Accessible to seekers.
arrangement_tips: Piano and full band. Build through verses. Very accessible melody. Works for seekers and believers alike.

title: Holy Forever
summary_theology: The eternal declaration of the seraphim that God is holy — holy, holy — becomes the anthem of the redeemed who join the never-ending chorus of heaven.
practical_worship_leadership: Very accessible, widely used. Creates deep atmosphere for adoration. Works for any tradition, any service.
arrangement_tips: Piano and full band. Slow build from intimate to powerful. The simplicity of the holy holy repetition is deeply effective.

title: Lion and the Lamb
summary_theology: Jesus as both Lion of Judah and Lamb of God; His dual nature as the all-powerful King and the sacrificial Redeemer is the paradox at the center of the gospel.
practical_worship_leadership: High energy, very well known. Works for any celebration service. Congregation sings the roaring declaration enthusiastically.
arrangement_tips: Full band, high energy. Build to full roar on chorus. Guitar and keys prominent. Repeat bridge multiple times.

title: See a Victory
summary_theology: The battle belongs to the Lord and He will bring victory; the believer declares what faith sees before it manifests, trusting in the God who fights on their behalf.
practical_worship_leadership: Excellent for breakthrough services or any warfare emphasis. Congregation declares victory before it is seen. Very empowering.
arrangement_tips: Piano and full band. Build through song. Chorus is anthemic. Long outro for declaration. Strong ending.

title: King of Kings
summary_theology: A theological sweep from eternity past to present: the pre-existent Word becomes flesh, walks to Calvary, rises victorious, and reigns forever as King of Kings.
practical_worship_leadership: Modern hymn. Works anywhere from Easter to Advent to Ascension. Congregation of all theological backgrounds engages deeply.
arrangement_tips: Piano-led, waltz feel. Four verses tell the full gospel narrative. Build from verse 1 to full band by verse 4.

title: My Redeemer Lives
summary_theology: Drawing on Job's desperate declaration in the midst of suffering, this song affirms with resurrection certainty: My Redeemer lives and will stand on this earth.
practical_worship_leadership: High energy, declaration. Works for any service. Very accessible, known widely. Great for Easter or general praise.
arrangement_tips: Full band, driving energy. Congregational declaration from the first chorus. Bridge adds power. Optional key change.

title: Shine Jesus Shine
summary_theology: A prayer for Christ's light to fill the earth through the church, with the Holy Spirit empowering believers to shine as witnesses in a dark world.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic Graham Kendrick praise anthem. Works for missions, Pentecost, or any service on light and witness.
arrangement_tips: Piano and full band. March-like energy. Congregation sings from first note. Very accessible. Works with flags and movement.

title: There Is a Fountain Filled with Blood
summary_theology: The fountain of Christ's blood, shed at Calvary, has power to cleanse every sin; those who wash in it are pardoned and prepared for glory.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic atonement hymn. Works for Good Friday, communion, or any cross-centered service. Very theologically rich.
arrangement_tips: Organ or piano, all verses for full arc. Can be arranged with a contemporary feel while retaining lyric power.

title: Victory in Jesus
summary_theology: Testimony of the overcoming victory found in Jesus — who healed, saved, and gave eternal life; this joy-filled declaration resonates across generations.
practical_worship_leadership: Beloved testimony hymn. Works for any celebration. Particularly popular in Southern and traditional churches. High participation.
arrangement_tips: Piano or organ, march feel. Southern Gospel arrangement is classic. Contemporary band can also carry it well.

title: Wonderful, Merciful Savior
summary_theology: The multifaceted character of Christ — wonderful, merciful, gracious, gentle, righteous, mighty — is celebrated in a tender waltz of personal adoration.
practical_worship_leadership: Gentle, tender. Works for intimate services or women's ministry. Beautiful melody that carries the lyric well.
arrangement_tips: Piano and strings. Gentle waltz feel. Works beautifully with women's choir or quartet. Unhurried, devotional pace.

title: Be unto Your Name
summary_theology: To God alone be all glory, honor, and praise; the response to His sovereignty and majesty is the total and joyful surrender of all honor to His name.
practical_worship_leadership: Works as a statement of Soli Deo Gloria. Good for any moment of collective adoration or covenant renewal.
arrangement_tips: Piano-led, gentle build. Works beautifully a cappella or with simple piano. Four-part harmony is excellent here.

title: Breathe
summary_theology: As God breathed life into Adam at creation, the Holy Spirit breathes life into the believer; the Christian is wholly dependent on this divine breath for spiritual life.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic intimate worship song. Very well known. Works for extended worship, prayer times, or Holy Spirit focus.
arrangement_tips: Piano or guitar, very simple. This song needs space. Congregation enters a meditative state naturally. Do not over-play.

title: Blessed Assurance
summary_theology: The testimony of complete assurance in salvation — the believer is purchased, forgiven, filled, and awaiting glory with the certainty of divine ownership.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the most beloved hymns ever written. Universal participation. Works for any tradition. Beloved by seniors and young alike.
arrangement_tips: Piano or organ, waltz feel. Contemporary arrangement also works well. All verses for full testimony arc.

title: My Hope Is Built on Nothing Less (Solid Rock)
summary_theology: All other ground is sinking sand; only the person and work of Christ provides a foundation that holds when the storms of life and the day of judgment come.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic that works everywhere. Works for stewardship, foundations, or assurance series. Congregation sings with conviction.
arrangement_tips: Piano or guitar. Contemporary arrangement of this hymn is particularly powerful. Build to full band on the chorus.

title: Great Is Thy Faithfulness
summary_theology: God's faithfulness is not occasional but great and inexhaustible; every morning brings fresh mercies from the God whose character never changes.
practical_worship_leadership: Perhaps the most beloved hymn on God's faithfulness. Works for any service. Universal participation across traditions.
arrangement_tips: Piano and organ, or contemporary full band. All verses develop the theology of faithful provision. Do not skip verses.

title: Amazing Grace (My Chains Are Gone)
summary_theology: The timeless lyric of John Newton combined with a modern chorus declaring that grace has broken every chain; old song made new, speaking to every generation.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the most-sung versions of Amazing Grace today. Works everywhere. Bridge chorus adds personal testimony dimension.
